title: Freelance Canva Template Designer
description:
WHAT FITBRAND IS

FitBrand is a monthly marketing subscription for small gym owners, personal trainers, and boutique fitness studios. Each month, subscribers receive a content system: a social media calendar, caption copy, and Canva templates they can customize and post themselves.



The templates are the product. Gym owners receive them, swap in their logo, brand colors, and photos, and post. Every template you build needs to look professional but be simple enough that a non-designer can edit without breaking anything.



PHASE 1 — BUILD THE TEMPLATE LIBRARY



Your first job is building a library of 30–50 Canva templates across three visual style directions. Each style targets a different gym audience type. You will receive a brief for each template specifying the post type, style, and purpose.



• STYLE 1: CLEAN & CONTEMPORARY

• STYLE 2: BOLD & EDGY

• STYLE 3: WARM COMMUNITY

• Target: Wellness-forward, middle-of-the-road members and the social crowd

• Target: Fire breathers, CrossFit, HYROX, hardcore athletes

• Target: Beginners, nervous starters, community-motivated members

• Light or white backgrounds, modern sans-serif fonts, minimal and breathable layouts

• Dark backgrounds — black, charcoal, deep navy. High contrast, aggressive typography

• Warm earthy tones, approachable photography-forward layouts, inviting energy

• Works across a wide range of gym brands with minimal effort to customize

• Bold and striking — looks premium even with minimal customization

• Soft and welcoming — built for gyms whose members aren’t all hardcore athletes





Template Types (mixed across all 3 styles):

• Generic motivational / quote posts

• Tip of the Week

• Testimonial / member spotlight

• Promotion or offer announcement

• Class or schedule highlight

• Community or culture post

• Challenge or event post

• Coach or team spotlight

• Seasonal and holiday posts — added in later phases



ONGOING WORK — AFTER PHASE 1



Once the core library is built, work continues monthly and expands into new formats:



• New template additions each month based on themes, seasons, and upcoming holidays

• Holiday and fitness event templates (New Year, Valentine’s Day, CrossFit Open, Murph, etc.)

• Flyers and event posters in letter and tabloid sizes

• Printable in-gym signage and promotional sheets

• Custom branded work if we launch higher-tier subscriber packages



This is meant to be a long-term working relationship. We’re building a real product and want a designer who grows with it.



CANVA & ACCESS REQUIREMENTS

This is non-negotiable: all design work happens inside our Canva account. You will be invited as an editor with folder-level access. You do not design in your own account and transfer files to us.



This protects our intellectual property and keeps the library organized in one place. I



You must know how to:

• Build clean, editable Canva templates with proper placeholder structure (colors, fonts, logos, photos)

• Set up templates so non-designers can customize them without breaking the layout

• Use Canva’s folder organization and template sharing features

• Size correctly for Instagram feed posts, stories, and



WHO WE’RE LOOKING FOR

Fitness knowledge matters as much as design skill. Our templates need to feel like they were made by someone who actually understands gym culture — not someone who found stock photos of dumbbells.



Strong fit if you:

• Are highly proficient in Canva — it is your primary design tool

• Understand American gym and fitness culture (CrossFit, boutique gyms, group fitness, personal training)

• Get the difference between a hardcore athlete, a group fitness regular, and a nervous first-timer — and can design for each

• Have a clean, modern design sensibility but can shift to bold and edgy when the brief calls for it

• Work independently from a brief without needing hand-holding

• Communicate clearly and flag problems early



Bonus if you:

• Have designed for fitness brands, gyms, or wellness businesses before

• Personally train, coach, or have a strong connection to fitness culture

• Have built template libraries or design systems before



HOW TO APPLY



Please include:

• 2–3 relevant design samples (fitness preferred — any social media template work is fine)

• A brief note on your fitness knowledge or personal connection to the industry

• Confirmation you are comfortable working inside our Canva account

• Your rate and an estimated timeline to complete a 30–50 template Phase 1 build



We look forward to working with you!

title: Sales Closer
description:
Construction Solutions Consultant (Project Closer)

Location: Los Angeles, CA (In-person & Zoom)



Compensation: $60,000 Base Salary + Uncapped Commission



The Opportunity

MID Construction is a premier design-build firm specializing in ground-up single-family and multifamily projects across Los Angeles. We have the internal team, the proven systems, and a steady pipeline of high-quality, pre-qualified leads.



What we are missing is the "Face of Construction"—a seasoned professional who can walk into a room (or a Zoom call) and provide the technical confidence homeowners and developers need to move forward with a multi-million dollar project.



This is not a "sales machine" role. This is a role for a builder who loves the strategy and relationship-building of pre-construction but is ready to step away from the daily grind of onsite management.



The Role: Technical Credibility, Not Hard Selling

You won't be cold calling or hunting for leads. Our internal team handles the lead generation, qualification, and scheduling. Your job is to step in once the client is ready to talk shop.



Key Responsibilities:



Technical Consultation: Lead deep-dive meetings with clients to review plans, discuss scope, and explain construction sequencing.



Relationship Building: Serve as the primary point of contact during the early engagement stages, ensuring architects, developers, and homeowners feel heard and understood.



Plan Review: Analyze blueprints and project complexity to provide high-level insights into how MID Construction will deliver the project.



Closing: Guide the negotiation and contract discussion with a calm, credible presence, moving projects from "proposal" to "signed contract."



Internal Collaboration: Work closely with our estimating and project management teams to ensure a seamless handoff once the deal is closed.



What You Won’t Do:

No cold outreach or lead generation.



No scheduling or administrative logistics.



No detailed estimating (handled by our internal estimators).



Who You Are

This role is perfect for a former General Contractor, Superintendent, Project Manager, or Estimator who is a natural communicator.



The Expert: You’ve been around construction long enough to answer technical questions off the cuff. You understand how large residential projects are priced and delivered.



The Consultant: You prefer building trust over "pitching." You come across as calm, professional, and highly credible.



The Strategist: You can navigate the nuances of the Los Angeles market and handle the differing needs of a first-time homeowner versus a seasoned developer.



The Closer: You enjoy the "win" of signing a new project and thrive in a results-oriented environment.



Qualifications

Experience: Minimum 5+ years of "boots on the ground" construction experience (GC, PM, or similar).



Technical Skills: Ability to read and interpret architectural and structural plans fluently.



Communication: Exceptional verbal and written skills; comfortable in high-stakes negotiations.



Location: Based in or near Los Angeles with the ability to meet clients in person as needed.



Why MID Construction?

We offer a stable $60,000 base salary to provide security, with a commission structure that rewards your ability to convert our steady stream of leads into active projects. You get the support of a full internal team so you can focus entirely on what you do best: building client trust and closing deals.



Ready to be the face of our next big build? [Insert Link to Apply / Contact
]

title: Marketing Operations Specialist (GoHighLevel + Meta Ads)
description:
MARKETING OPERATIONS SPECIALIST - GOHIGHLEVEL & META ADS



Company: LYNKS AI Agency (Digital Marketing Agency, Toronto, Canada)

Position: Full-Time Marketing Operations Specialist

Location: Remote (Philippines preferred)

Rate: $1,100-$2,000 CAD/month (based on experience)

Hours: 40 hours/week, Monday-Friday



ABOUT US



LYNKS AI Agency specializes in marketing and lead generation for real estate professionals and property management companies. We build complete marketing systems using GoHighLevel, run Meta advertising campaigns, and help our clients generate consistent leads.



We're growing fast (5 clients, adding more monthly) and need someone to handle technical buildouts and creative execution so our founder can focus on strategy and client relationships.



WHAT YOU'LL DO



Technical Buildouts (60% of time):



• Build websites in GoHighLevel (5-10 pages per client)

• Set up CRM workflows, automations, and
 sequences

• Integrate GoHighLevel with other platforms (LeadSimple, Zapier, Meta Ads)

• Create lead capture forms and landing pages

• Configure pipelines, tags, and contact management systems

Creative & Advertising (40% of time):



• Design Meta ad creative (static images, carousel ads) using Canva

• Write ad copy for real estate/property management campaigns

• Build landing pages that convert

• Create
 templates and marketing materials

• Manage Meta Ads campaigns (setup, optimization, reporting)



REQUIREMENTS



Must Have:



• 2+ years experience with GoHighLevel (building websites, workflows, integrations)

• Meta Ads experience (campaign setup, targeting, optimization)

• Fluent English (written and spoken)

• Design skills (Canva, Photoshop, or similar)

• Reliable internet and able to work Canadian business hours (8am-5pm EST)

Nice to Have:

• Experience with AI creation & Higgsfiled & Nanobanna 





• Zapier /Make integration experience

• Video editing skills



WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR



You're the right fit if you:



• Can work independently without constant direction

• Take ownership of projects from start to finish

• Ask questions when you're stuck (don't spin your wheels)

• Communicate proactively (daily updates, flag problems early)

• Care about quality and pay attention to details

• Can manage multiple client projects simultaneously



COMPENSATION & BENEFITS



• Pay: $1,100-$2,000 CAD/month (paid monthly via PayPal)

• Hours: 40 hours/week, flexible within Canadian business hours

• Growth: As the agency grows, this role can expand (team lead, project manager, etc.)

• Stability: Long-term position for the right person



HOW TO APPLY



Send a message with:



1. Portfolio/Examples:  • 2-3 GoHighLevel websites you've built (live links or screenshots)

  • 2-3 Meta ad campaigns you've managed (screenshots with results)

  • Any design work (ads, landing pages, graphics)



2. Answers to these questions:  • How many years of GoHighLevel experience do you have?

  • What's the most complex GHL integration you've built?

  • Describe a Meta ad campaign you optimized - what was the result?

  • What's your availability? Can you work 8am-5pm EST?



3. Short intro:  • Why you're interested in this role

  • What makes you a great fit



Subject line: "Marketing Ops Specialist - [Your Name]"



We'll review applications within 3-5 days and reach out to top candidates for an interview.



- Austin Furtado, Founder

LYNKS AI Agency

title: Executive Assistant
description:
We’re a custom upholstery and window shade company looking for an experienced Executive Assistant / Project Coordinator who already understands how this industry works: quotes, measurements, materials, lead times, production timelines, installation scheduling, and customer updates.



This role is equal parts admin, customer communication, and project tracking. You’ll help keep projects moving from inquiry to installation by staying on top of details, vendors, and timelines.



Key Responsibilities

 • Manage project flow: lead ? consult/measure ? quote ? deposit ? production ? install ? completion

 • Coordinate scheduling for measurements, site visits, and installations

 • Communicate with customers about timelines, approvals, deposits, and updates 
/phone/text)

 • Follow up with vendors/workrooms on materials, hardware, fabrics, and lead times

 • Track all project details accurately (measurements, fabric selections, hardware, special notes)

 • Prepare and organize quotes/invoices/POs and ensure approvals are documented

 • Keep a clean tracker so every project has a clear status, next step, and due date

 • Support the owner/team with calendar management, reminders, and priority follow-ups

 • Create/check simple checklists to reduce mistakes (wrong fabric, wrong hardware, missed notes)



Requirements (Must Have)

 • Prior experience in custom upholstery and/or window treatments (shades/blinds/drapery)

 • Familiarity with the full order lifecycle: quoting, materials, lead times, scheduling, installation coordination

 • Strong written and spoken English; professional customer communication

 • Excellent attention to detail and organization (measurements, selections, hardware, timelines)

 • Comfortable with spreadsheets and tracking systems (Google Sheets/Excel; CRM/project tools)

 • Reliable computer, stable internet, and quiet space for calls



Nice to Have

 • Experience supporting an interior design studio, workroom, showroom, or installer network

 • QuickBooks experience (or invoicing tools)

 • Familiarity with common shade types/hardware terminology and fabric sourcing workflows

 • Experience building SOPs/checklists



What Success Looks Like (30–60 Days)

 • Quotes go out faster with fewer errors

 • Customers get proactive updates and fewer “checking in” messages

 • Lead times and vendor follow-ups are tracked consistently

 • Install schedules run smoothly with fewer last-minute surprises

 • The owner/team has a clear view of every project’s status at any time



Schedule

 • Remote

 • Full-time preferred

 • Must overlap with the company’s operating hours (shared during screening)

9-5 EST

title: Administrative Assistant
description:
We’re hiring an Administrative Assistant who’s not only organized, but also comfortable working with transaction records and documents. A big part of this job is helping us review transactions, keep things clean and documented, and flag anything that doesn’t make sense.



You don’t need to be an accountant—but you should be the type of person who looks at a transaction and thinks: “What is this? Why did it happen? Does it match the story and the paperwork?”



What You’ll Do



Keep schedules, files, and documents organized (digital + any physical paperwork)



Help review transaction lists and supporting documents (bank activity, receipts, invoices, payment confirmations)



Categorize and label transactions clearly so the team can understand them quickly



Flag transactions that look unusual, inconsistent, duplicated, or missing documentation



Follow up internally (and sometimes with vendors) to confirm what a transaction is and why it happened



Prepare simple summaries for management (e.g., “these 12 transactions need clarification”)



Handle general admin work:
, calls, forms, basic spreadsheets, scanning/uploading, etc.



What We’re Looking For



Strong attention to detail (you naturally catch things others miss)



Comfortable working with spreadsheets and lists (Excel or Google Sheets)



Clear communication and confidence asking questions when something doesn’t add up



Professional handling of confidential information



1+ year in admin, operations, office support, or similar

title: Operations & Customer Support Coordinator
description:
We’re looking for an organized, calm, highly accountable operations coordinator to manage day-to-day customer communication and delivery logistics for a residential appliances business. You’ll handle customer updates, scheduling, shipment/delivery issues, driver coordination, and escalation resolution—owning problems end-to-end until they’re closed.



This is a remote role with a lot of moving parts. If you like structure, clear priorities, and being the person who makes everything run on time, this is a strong fit.



Key Responsibilities

 • Serve as the main point of contact for customers regarding delivery scheduling, updates, delays, and issues

 • Coordinate deliveries with drivers/dispatch and internal teams (reschedules, routing changes, special instructions)

 • Track shipments/deliveries from “scheduled” to “completed” and keep status accurate in the system

 • Resolve escalations: late deliveries, damaged items, missing items, wrong address/notes, driver delays/no-shows

 • Communicate clearly and professionally with customers by phone, SMS, and

 • Maintain a clean tracker (tickets/CRM/spreadsheet) with next steps, owners, and timelines

 • Proactively identify problems before they happen (confirmation calls, route checks, missing info)

 • Create simple SOPs/checklists as you notice recurring issues



Requirements

 • 2+ years in operations coordination, dispatch, logistics coordination, or customer support in a fast-paced environment

 • Strong spoken and written English (phone support required)

 • Proven ability to prioritize and stay calm under pressure

 • Very strong attention to detail (addresses, appointment windows, notes, order details)

 • Comfortable using tools like CRMs/ticketing systems, Google Sheets/Excel, and chat/email daily

 • Reliable computer, stable internet, and a quiet work environment suitable for calls



Nice to Have

 • Experience with last-mile delivery, home services, appliances, furniture delivery, or field service operations

 • Experience with Zendesk/Freshdesk/HubSpot/Front (or similar)

 • Experience managing multiple drivers/routes per day

 • Experience writing SOPs and improving workflows



What Success Looks Like (30–60 Days)

 • Customers receive proactive updates and fewer “where is my delivery?” calls

 • Fewer missed appointments and smoother reschedules

 • Delivery issues are documented clearly, escalations handled quickly, and root causes tracked

 • Tracking system stays accurate and leadership always knows the status at a glance



Schedule

 • Remote

 • Full-time preferred

 • Must be available during business hours aligned with the team’s operating timezone (details shared during screening)

title: Property Manager
description:
Blackacre Property Management is seeking a motivated and organized Property Manager to oversee the day-to-day operations of our properties. This role focuses on administrative and operational management, vendor coordination, and financial responsibilities. 



Hours - 9 am - 6 pm



Key Responsibilities:

• Maintain and update the rent roll, ensuring accuracy and timely reporting.

• Manage tenant work orders, coordinating repairs and maintenance with vendors.

• Send notices to tenants as required (e.g., policy updates, maintenance alerts, or other

communications).

• Obtain quotes and coordinate maintenance or repair work with contractors.

• Address and resolve issues related to property operations, tenant concerns, or vendor

work.

• Maintain accurate records of property operations and work order tracking.

• Ensure properties remain compliant with company policies and applicable regulations.



Required: Must be available to respond to emergency work orders, either by phone or via
, and take immediate action by contacting the appropriate vendor. For example, in the case of a water leak at night, a plumber must be dispatched to the site promptly. In the meantime, use practical solutions or AI tools to provide guidance to the tenant—such as placing a bucket under the leak—until professional assistance arrives.



Qualifications:

• Speaks fluent English and is understandle as they have to speak to our tennats. 

Bachelor’s degree in Business or related field preferred.

• Strong organizational and communication skills.

• Ability to manage multiple tasks and prioritize effectively.

• REQUIRED - Proficiency with office software and AppFolio property management.

title: Crochet Artist
description:
We're looking to hire an experienced crochet artist to help review personalized crochet patterns of pets.



What the role involves:

1. Selecting the appropriate yarn colors from our inventory to match the pet shown in a reference photo

2. Updating the pattern to reflect the necessary color changes and markings based on the pet photo

3. Occasionally, crocheting sections to confirm the pattern works as written

4. Sharing notes and feedback



Projects are small amigurumi animals and typically use a 4mm hook with worsted-weight yarn.



Ideally looking for someone who:



- Has experience with amigurumi

- Is comfortable reading and reviewing patterns

- Has an eye for stitch counts and color changes



This will be PT to FT work for the right person. If interested, please comment or send me a message with:



- Your crochet experience

- Any amigurumi work you’ve made

- Your availability



You can review our website here: 



Thanks so much!

title: Video Editor for E-Commerce Ads (Full-Time, Long-Term Role)
description:
Video Editor Purpose:



The purpose of the Video Editor Role for our brand is to process ad briefs from our creative team and bring them to life through the power of video editing.



As a Video Editor, you are responsible for the actual execution & applying the finishing touches on all the video ads we create. Our success as a marketing team will rely on your ability to comprehend each ad concept, strategically put the pieces together, and enhance the overall marketing message with your edits.



You understand that the smallest details can make the biggest differences. So, you take pride in paying immense attention to detail in everything you do as those details can be the difference between an ad that is shown to millions & an ad that is shown to very few.



Your active purpose at our company is to work alongside our Creative Strategists to edit & bring to life ad briefs, utilize our creative resources, and help produce winning ads month over month.





Video Editor Primary Roles:



- Edit 5-10 ad briefs per week

- Upload & organize edited ads into specific folders with the correct resolutions

- Communicate with creative strategists to ensure ad concepts are executed correctly

- Listen & apply feedback from the creative team for existing and new ads

- Learn and understand what makes “Winning” & “Losing” ad concepts





Qualifications & Character Traits:



- 1 Year of experience in video editing or designing ads

- 1 Year of experience in Video Editing tools like CapCut, Adobe

- 1 Year of experience editing eCommerce ads

- Know how to use AI like Heygen, Hedra, Elevenlabs, Runway, NanoBanana etc. (this is crucial since almost all of our ads are made with AI)

- Working in a team environment & understanding individual roles

Bonus: Experience working at another DTC ecommerce brand with DR focused ads. 





CHARACTER TRAITS:



- Excruciatingly high level of attention to detail with all designs & edits

- Artistic & creative oriented mindset

- Visual communicator & storyteller

- High curiosity & an active imagination

- Strong willingness to do the ‘boring’ work required

- Great communicator with the team

- Time-efficient, but willing to spend extra time to do things right

- Coachable & desire to grow skill set & personal traits





This is NOT For You If:



- You are not a creative person

- You do not enjoy editing content

- You do not have creative skills

- You do not like a high-operating environment

- You are unorganized

- You are not a team player

- You do not understand eCommerce

- You are not passionate about growth





Hours:



M-F 10:00 AM - 6:00 PM AEDT (Flexibile)





Growth Potential:



At our company, we highly value growth and development in all areas.

With great performance, the potential for you as a Video Editor is significant:

- Get priority on editing ads & receive the bulk of requests.

- Creative Strategist role to lead and manage other editors & focus on high-level strategy

title: Video Editor - Performance Ad Creative
description:
Mr Simple is an Australian menswear brand known for timeless, well-made clothing. We're scaling our digital advertising and need a fast, reliable video editor to help us produce high volumes of ad creative for Meta 
/Instagram), TikTok, and other digital channels.

This role is about speed and volume — not cinematic filmmaking. You'll be turning raw footage and assets into scroll-stopping short-form ads, day in, day out.



What You'll Be Doing



Editing short-form video ads (15–60 seconds) for Meta, TikTok, and Instagram Reels

Creating multiple variations of ads — swapping hooks, CTAs, text overlays, and music

Resizing and reformatting videos across placements (9:16, 1:1, 4:5)

Working from creative briefs and reference examples provided by the team

Adding text overlays, captions, and basic motion graphics

Turning around edits quickly — we move fast and test a lot of creative

Organising and managing video assets and project files

title: SDR / Appointment Setter – Short-Form Content & CRO Agency
description:
Looking for a high-energy "Closer" to help scale a Short-Form Content & CRO Agency.

We help local businesses dominate social media with high-retention video content and conversion rate optimization (CRO). We are growing fast and need a dedicated Sales Development Representative (SDR) to hunt for new opportunities and book meetings.



What you will be doing:

• Cold Calling: Reaching out to local business owners (Gyms, Med-Spas, Real Estate, etc.).

• Lead Generation: Identifying businesses with poor social media presence or "dead" websites.

• Appointment Setting: Your only goal is to get a "Yes" for a 15-minute discovery call.

What we offer:

• Competitive Base Pay: $4.00 – $7.00 USD per hour (depending on experience).

• Aggressive Commissions: $20.00 bonus for every "showed up" meeting + a % of closed deals.

• Growth: Opportunity to move into a Head of Sales role as we scale.

• Training: We provide the scripts and the leads; you provide the energy.

Requirements (Non-Negotiable):



1. Flawless English: You must have a clear, professional, and persuasive speaking voice.



2. Thick Skin: You aren't afraid of "No" and can handle gatekeepers with ease.



3. Quiet Workspace: High-quality headset and zero background noise.



4. Reliability: This is a long-term partnership, not a "gig."



HOW TO APPLY (Read Carefully):

To prove you have attention to detail, please follow these steps:

1. Start your application message with the word "HOOK."

2. Attach a 30-60 second voice recording (use Vocaroo or similar) pitching me on why I should hire you over 100 other applicants.

3. Tell me about your most successful "cold call" win from a previous job.

Applications without a voice recording will be ignored.

title: Property Management Assistant VA (Australian Real Estate, Dayshift, Remote))
description:
Work From Home | Australian Hours | Supportive & Professional Team



About the Role:

We’re seeking a reliable and detail-oriented Property Management Assistant from the Philippines to support a busy property management team in Victoria, Australia. This role is ideal for someone with a background in property management or administration who enjoys keeping things organised and ensuring compliance.



What You’ll Do:

- Send tenant notices and process rental applications

- Prepare lease documents and liaise with trades for maintenance and repairs

- Check compliance in folders and CRMs to ensure all documents are up to date

- Manage and update the property database and CRM systems

- Organise pre-listing kits and marketing materials such as property ads and brochures

- Communicate with clients, tenants, and tradespeople as necessary

- Write property descriptions for ads and brochures

- Manage calendars and
s to keep tasks on track



What We’re Looking For:

- Property management experience in Victoria Austalia is a MUST

- Real estate experience in general is essential

- Strong attention to detail and organizational skills

- Excellent written and spoken English communication skills

- Proactive, reliable, and able to follow instructions independently

- Familiarity with CRMs, database management, and property marketing tools preferred



What You’ll Get:

- Paid Australian public holidays

- Bi-weekly pay via Wise

- Supportive and professional team atmosphere

- No graveyard shifts

Do not reply to this post.

Job Code: PMAVA

Apply directly through this link:

title: Social Media Video Editor (Instagram, TikTok & YouTube)
description:
We are an Australian wellness brand looking for a social media video editor who understands Instagram Reels, TikTok and YouTube Shorts.



This role is more than basic editing. You should understand hooks, pacing and storytelling for social media, and be able to suggest ideas for how videos can be structured. Responsibilities are:

• Edit short form videos for Instagram Reels, TikTok and YouTube Shorts

• Turn raw footage into engaging social content

• Add captions, hooks and pacing suited to social media

• Occasionally edit longer YouTube style videos

• Suggest ideas for video edits and content structure



Our brand is a natural wellness brand selling to Australia, so edits must feel clean, subtle and lifestyle-driven. The content should feel natural and authentic, similar to modern wellness and lifestyle brands.



We are NOT looking for:

• flashy edits

• heavy motion graphics

• 
 or gaming style editing



Job Requirements:

• Experience editing short form vertical videos

• Strong understanding of Instagram, TikTok or YouTube content

• Excellent English communication skills

• Portfolio of previous work



Bonus if you have worked with wellness, beauty, lifestyle or fitness brands.



Application Instructions - Important! 

Please include:

• Links to 3 videos you have edited

• Links to 2 social media accounts whose editing style you like

• Applications without examples will not be considered.

• Please include the phrase Glow State Editor in the first sentence of your application so we know you read this job post.

title: Bookkeeper/Accountant
description:
Role

Handle bookkeeping and accounting tasks for multiple clients while working with the internal team.



Responsibilities

• Manage bookkeeping for multiple clients

• Use QuickBooks Online (QBO)

• Reconcile bank and credit card accounts

• Prepare monthly financial reports

• Support payroll and GST/HST tasks

• Communicate with clients and CRA when needed



Requirements

• Experience with QuickBooks Online

• Organized and able to handle multiple tasks

• Responsible and detail-oriented

• Strong teamwork and communication

• Available to work Vancouver time (PST)

title: Experienced Client & Event Coordinator
description:
Authority On Demand is the premier authority-building firm for founders, CEOs, and category leaders who refuse to be ordinary. Recently recognized on the 2025 Inc. 5000 as #146 nationally and #1 in Public Relations & Publishing, we engineer "Category of One" status for the world’s most influential voices.



We are looking for a Client & Event Coordinator to join our elite team and manage the seamless execution of these high-stakes opportunities.



Role Overview:



As a Client & Event Coordinator, you will be the vital link between our high-profile clients (CEOs, entrepreneurs, and thought leaders) and global event organizers. You will manage professional logistics, coordinate keynote details, and ensure smooth communication so that our clients experience a world-class journey from the green room to the main stage.



This role requires working in the EST time zone to align with our North American clients and media partners.



Key Responsibilities



1. Conduct professional calls with clients and event organizers.

2. Coordinate event details, schedules, and requirements between both parties.

3. Act as the primary point of contact for updates, queries, and clarifications.

4. Maintain detailed records of communication and provide status reports to the management team.

5. Ensure smooth client experiences by proactively resolving coordination issues.

6. Uphold a high standard of verbal and written English communication at all times.



Requirements



- Excellent command of spoken and written English.

- Strong communication and interpersonal skills.

- Prior experience in client coordination, event management, or customer-facing roles preferred.

- Ability to multitask, prioritize, and stay organized under pressure.

- Professional phone etiquette and ability to handle calls confidently.

- Proactive, detail-oriented, and reliable.

- Must be available to work during EST working hours.



What We Offer



- Competitive compensation package.

- Opportunity to work with international clients and global event organizers.

- A dynamic, mission-driven work environment.

- Growth opportunities within a fast-scaling company.



How to Apply



If you are passionate about communication, coordination, and client success, we’d love to hear from you!



Apply by sharing your CV and a short cover letter and A SHORT VIDEO INTRO.

title: Python Programmer/Developer
description:
Are you an experienced Python Developer looking to take your career to new heights? Look no further! We're an innovative company based in the US, and we're on the hunt for a seasoned Python Programmer/Developer to become a pivotal part of our team. This is a golden opportunity to nurture a versatile and fulfilling career.



We pay high performers and great problem solvers. With excellent Performance, you will get an increased rate.



We generously reward high achievers and innovative problem solvers. Get ready to excel with our comprehensive paid training!



Job Summary: As a Full Stack Developer, you will be responsible for designing, developing, and maintaining web applications and software solutions. You will work on both the front-end and back-end of projects, collaborating with cross-functional teams to deliver high-quality and user-friendly applications. Your expertise in multiple programming languages, databases, and development frameworks will be essential to drive the success of our projects.



Job Brief

We are seeking a Full-Stack Python Developer who can own projects end-to-end—from backend logic and API integrations to front-end design and implementation. This role is ideal for someone who thrives in a fast-paced environment, enjoys problem-solving, and wants to build tools that make a tangible impact both internally (automation, data pipelines, reporting) and externally (client dashboards, web apps, portals)



Requirements:

At least 6 years of work experience

Strong proficiency in Python (Django, Flask, or FastAPI experience a plus).

Solid experience with front-end development (HTML, CSS, JavaScript).

Experience integrating with RESTful APIs and handling data transformations.

Strong understanding of database design and queries (PostgreSQL, MySQL, or similar).

Knowledge of Git/GitHub for version control.

Ability to translate non-technical requirements into technical implementation.

Comfortable using AI tools such as Cursor or Github CoPilot to accelerate development



Preferred Skills:

Experience with website builders (Hubspot, Clickfunnels, Webflow, or similar)

Experience in financial services, fintech, or consulting industries.

Familiarity with cloud deployment platforms (AWS, Azure, or Pythonanywhere).

Knowledge of modern UI frameworks and styling libraries (Bootstrap, Tailwind, Bulma, etc.).

Experience with task automation/orchestration tools (Celery, Airflow, Zapier, etc.).

Experience with Pandas, Numpy, and other data science libraries

Understanding of security best practices in handling financial data



Night shift 

Full Time



APPLY HERE:

title: YouTube Thumbnail Designer (Viral Style)
description:
A Note Before You Apply:



This is your first test. We do not consider generic, copy-and-pasted applications. If you don’t follow the instructions below precisely, your application will be archived. We’re looking for tea
mbers who are detail-oriented and committed, and that starts here.



You can find our founder on Instagram 
 to learn more about who you’d be working with.





TYPE OF WORK: Project-Based



WAGE / SALARY: Highly Competitive



VOLUME: 12+ Thumbnails Per Month



JOB OVERVIEW



We are looking for a creative and skilled YouTube Thumbnail Designer who specializes in creating high-energy, "viral style" thumbnails. The ideal candidate has a strong portfolio of eye-catching designs that drive clicks and a deep understanding of what makes a thumbnail successful on YouTube.



You will be responsible for designing thumbnails that are not only visually appealing but also strategically crafted to capture attention and communicate the video's value. We need a designer who can work quickly, meet a 48-hour turnaround time, and consistently produce high-quality work for a minimum of 12 videos per month.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES



•

Design high-impact, "viral style" YouTube thumbnails that increase click-through rates.



•

Develop creative concepts and compelling text for thumbnails, sometimes using only an audio file for context.



•

Ensure all thumbnails align with our brand's visual identity and high standards.



•

Meet a 48-hour turnaround time for each thumbnail design.



•

Collaborate with our content team to ensure thumbnails accurately represent the video content.



APPLICATION INSTRUCTIONS



To apply, you must follow the instructions below precisely. Failure to follow these instructions will result in immediate disqualification.



1.

Send an
 to 

Use the subject line: Viral Thumbnail Editor



3.

Inside the
, copy and paste the questions below, providing your answer after each one:



A. Please provide a link to your portfolio, specifically showcasing your best YouTube thumbnail designs.



B. What is your price per thumbnail?



C. What is your desired hourly rate?



D. Are you able to listen to an audio file of a YouTube video and come up with the thumbnail text and concept yourself? Please describe your process for this.



E. What is your typical turnaround time for a single thumbnail design?



F. Are you comfortable working in a Christian-based environment?

title: Creative Short-Form Video Editor (Reels/TikToks/Shorts)
description:
A Note Before You Apply:



This is your first test. We do not consider generic, copy-and-pasted applications. If you don’t follow the instructions below precisely, your application will be archived. We’re looking for tea
mbers who are detail-oriented and committed, and that starts here.



You can find our founder on Instagram 
 to learn more about who you’d be working with.



Creative Short-Form Video Editor (Reels/TikToks/Shorts)

TYPE OF WORK:



Full-Time



WAGE / SALARY:



Highly Competitive



HOURS PER WEEK:



40



JOB OVERVIEW



We are a high-performing, Christian-based media team looking for an experienced and creative Short-Form Video Editor. We need a storyteller who can identify viral 
nts in long-form content and transform them into compelling short-form videos for platforms like Instagram Reels, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts.



This role is about more than just basic editing; we want a creator who understands how to build a powerful hook, find the core message, and bring it to life with skill and creativity.



We reward excellence with performance-based bonuses for high-view-count videos and for work that requires minimal revisions.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES



Analyze long-form video content to identify the most impactful and shareable 
nts.



Create viral-potential short-form videos that feature strong hooks, animated text, strategic zooms, and modern editing effects.



Adhere to our creative guideline of using only raw footage (no stock footage).



Develop engaging titles and thumbnails to maximize click-through rates.



Edit for narrative clarity, pacing, and storytelling—not just aesthetics.



REQUIREMENTS



Must be fluent in English to prevent spelling errors and ensure clear communication in tea
etings.



Demonstrated experience creating successful short-form video content for Instagram, TikTok, and/or YouTube Shorts.



Ability to work quickly and creatively with a high degree of autonomy.



Exceptional and prompt communication is a non-negotiable requirement.



Experience editing content for sales, business, or podcasts is a significant plus.



PERKS & CULTURE



Generous bonuses for top-performing content.



Additional compensation for edits that require few to no revisions.



Clear opportunities for rapid promotion within the team.

mbers like family.

 to

You've been warned ;) 



You can find me on instagram if you're looking so you know who you'd be working with :) 




 



TYPE OF WORK: Full-Time



WAGE / SALARY: Highly Competitive



HOURS PER WEEK: 30+



JOB OVERVIEW AND INSTRUCTIONS



We are seeking a highly skilled Senior Podcast Video Editor to join our Christian-based media team. This role is ideal for a professional experienced in managing large video files (50–70GB per camera angle) and delivering high-quality, long-form content for high-level entrepreneurs. You will be responsible for:



•

Combining and syncing multi-camera video footage with corresponding audio tracks.



•

Strategically switching between camera angles to align with the conversation's flow and enhance viewer engagement.



•

Crafting cinematic, high-quality intros from podcast highlights that generate curiosity, build suspense, and deliver immediate value.



•

Meeting fast turnaround times, with a minimum of four podcast edits required per month.



•

Exporting high-resolution video and audio files in all standard formats.



This position requires a powerful computer, high-speed internet, and a proven track record in podcast editing. We value efficiency and offer speed bonuses for urgent projects. Our team is committed to a positive, faith-based work environment where we support our members, pay on time (often in advance), and operate with integrity.



APPLICATION INSTRUCTIONS

 your application to

 and provide thorough answers:

•

title: ACCOUNTANTS
description:
FULL-TIME NIGHT SHIFT | Remote | For High-Performance Professionals Only



Pivotl is seeking an exceptional Remote Accountant to join our specialized team providing advanced accounting services across the United States. This is a FULL-TIME, NIGHT SHIFT position designed for seasoned professionals who thrive under pressure and deliver excellence consistently.



IMPORTANT: This is NOT a side job or flexible arrangement. We need dedicated professionals accustomed to night shift operations who can commit to full-time hours. If you're seeking part-time work or are uncomfortable with night schedules, please do not apply.



What Sets This Role Apart

- 4-day work week, 3 days off - Maximize your work-life balance

- Premium compensation for proven high performers

- Comprehensive benefits: Paid vacation/sick leave, extensive paid training, generous Christmas bonus

- Complete autonomy in a remote environment - perfect for self-directed professionals

- Specialized focus on HOME SERVICE industries (HVAC, Solar, and related sectors)



The Challenge - Are You Ready?



We need a financial data cleanup specialist who can handle:

- Granular-level financial data reconciliation across complex business structures (Profit & Loss, Balance Sheet, AR, Vendor Recon)

- High-pressure deadline management while maintaining 100?curacy

- Independent problem-solving of complex accounting discrepancies

- Proactive identification and resolution of financial irregularities

- Core Responsibilities for Elite Performers

- Execute comprehensive book-cleanup projects with tight deadlines

- Perform detailed adjusting entries with a deep understanding of tax implications

- Master QuickBooks Online for complex multi-entity structures

- Conduct advanced data analysis using Excel and Google Sheets

- Manage full-cycle AR/AP, invoicing, payroll, and bank reconciliations

- Deliver precise categorization and accounting across diverse business models



Non-Negotiable Requirements

- Proven track record in US accounting with demonstrable results

- Expert-level proficiency in QuickBooks Online

- Advanced Excel/Google Sheets skills for complex data manipulation

- Exceptional English communication - written and verbal

- Night shift availability - this is not flexible

- Autonomous work style - you must be self-directed and proactive

- Competitive Advantages (Preferred)

- Industry background in HVAC, solar, generators, roofing, foundations, or electrical

- Construction/contracting industry experience highly valued

- Experience using any of the following: Service Titan, Housecall Pro, Sera, Jobber, Service Trade, Acculynx, etc

- Experience using Keeper.app



Who Should Apply

- Rock stars only. We want professionals who:

- Thrive in high-pressure, deadline-driven environments

- Work autonomously without constant supervision

- Have extensive experience cleaning up complex historical financial data

- Take ownership of results and deliver consistent excellence

- Are committed to full-time night shift operations



Who Should NOT Apply

- Those seeking part-time or flexible arrangements

- Professionals are uncomfortable with night shift schedules

- Anyone looking for a "side job" or supplemental income

- Those requiring extensive hand-holding or micromanagement

- Ready to prove you're among the elite?



Apply Here: 

Pivotl - Where exceptional professionals build exceptional careers.

title: Program Analyst, Project Manager
description:
• 2+ years of project management experience required



Job Summary

Seeking a Program Analyst to provide full-time project management support on a national cancer registry effort supporting the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA). In this role, the Analyst will support the Project Mangers planning, coordinating, and executing a large-scale effort providing Oncology Data Specialist–Credentialed (ODS-C) support to address VA’s backlog of oncology cases.



Primary Responsibilities

The Program Associate will support the Program Managers in vetting ODS-C candidates, ensuring timely onboarding, supporting access to VA’s EMR and abstracting software, providing detailed monthly reports, and contributing to process improvement.



Minimum Qualifications

Qualifications

• 2+ years of project management experience

• Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field (e.g., public health, health sciences, business administration, project management, or a related discipline).

• Demonstrated ability to support large, cross-functional teams, including communications professionals and technical staff



Desired Qualifications

• Familiarity with VA systems, policies, and directives



Candidates that do not meet the required experience need not apply.

title: Restaurant Operations & Marketing VA
description:
We are seeking a highly organized and proactive Hospitality Virtual Assistant to support a growing restaurant business. This role will assist with operations documentation, marketing execution, and administrative organization to help streamline internal processes and improve brand visibility.

The ideal candidate is detail-oriented, tech-savvy, and comfortable supporting a fast-paced hospitality environment. This position works directly with ownership to develop systems, maintain documentation, and support marketing initiatives.

title: Billing Specialist
description:
Oversee the financial well being of one of our 9 branches. This includes making outbound calls to our customers that are behind on payments, Our outreach methods include sms,
, and voi
s messages.

title: Integrations Associate – Automation & Systems
description:
DAP Brands is seeking a detail-oriented, technically savvy, and solution-driven Integrations Associate to support our mission of building seamless, scalable automations across the organization. This role goes beyond task execution. You’ll work closely with our Technology Integration Manager to design, maintain, and optimize workflows that improve efficiency across Sales, Marketing, and Operations.



If you enjoy connecting systems, solving process gaps, and leveraging automation tools to create reliable, intelligent workflows, this role is for you.



Key Responsibilities:

- Integration Setup & Maintenance: Assist in setting up, testing, launching, and maintaining integrations using platforms such as Zapier, Make, and GoHighLevel. Monitor automations for accuracy and performance, troubleshoot issues, and implement long-term fixes.

- Automation & Workflow Optimization: Identify inefficiencies in existing processes and propose smart automation solutions. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to understand needs and build tailored workflows.

- Technical Support: Work with APIs, webhooks, and basic scripting (HTML, CSS, JavaScript, SQL) to resolve data sync issues, system errors, and performance bottlenecks.

- Documentation & Training: Document workflows, integrations, and processes to ensure scalability. Assist in training internal users on newly built systems and automation tools.



Tools You’ll Work With:

- Automation & Integrations: Zapier, Make, Webhooks

- CRMs & Operations Tools: GoHighLevel, ClickFunnels, Stripe, ClickUp

- Collaboration Tools: Google Sheets, Zoom, Google Workspace

- Scripting & APIs: HTML, CSS, JavaScript, SQL



Application Instructions:

Please submit your application through the online form provided: 
/careers



- To be considered, you MUST type “MAVERICK” in front of your full name if you are applying via our website: 
/careers

- If you are an Integrations Associate who takes ownership, thinks in systems, and thrives in a fast-moving, automation-driven environment, we invite you to apply.

title: US Estimator - WFH
description:
About PW USA:



At ProfitWorks, we believe that our people are the heart of our success. We are passionate about creating an environment where everyone feels valued, supported, and excited to grow. We’re looking for an Estimator Administrator to join our team to work directly with our client in the USA. Our team has two tea
mbers that have been with the founder for over ten years. Multiple others are nearing the five-year mark. This client is highly respected in the painting industry with national accolades and known for an incredible culture.



What we do:



We are a recruitment firm serving the home service and trades industry. Our referrals are all word of mouth, we have a client waiting list for onboarding. This is our fifth-year, and we still have every client we started the business with. We do things differently. It’s all about relationships and serving with excellence. We build and create solutions for our clients to be assisted with critical functions in their business.



Job Overview



Position: US Estimator



Salary: $1,200 - $1,300USD/month



Schedule: Philippines Graveyard Shift (8AM - 5PM EST Monday - Friday)



Location: Remote (100% Permanent WFH)



Job role:



As an Estimator, you will play a crucial role in accurately estimating costs and ensuring the success and profitability of our clients' projects. You're responsible for preparing accurate and competitive cost estimates for thermal and moisture protection scopes, including roofing, waterproofing, insulation, fireproofing, and building envelope systems. This role involves analyzing project plans, specifications, and related documents to determine material, labor, and equipment costs, and working closely with project managers, subcontractors, and suppliers. Your expertise in quantity takeoffs, cost estimation, vendor coordination, and meticulous documentation will contribute to the overall efficiency and excellence of our projects.



Key Qualifications:



Organization and Time Management: Strong organizational skills to manage multiple projects simultaneously, meet deadlines, and prioritize tasks effectively.



Experience: A minimum of 2 years of US experience as an Estimator or in a similar role within the construction industry.



Construction Knowledge: Strong understanding of commercial construction processes, materials, and methods..

Blueprint Reading: Proficiency in reading and interpreting construction plans, specifications, and related documents.



Takeoff Software: Experience with construction takeoff software tools, such as Bluebeam, On-Screen Takeoff, Procore Estimating, or similar applications.



Attention to Detail: Strong attention to detail to perform accurate quantity takeoffs and cost estimates, leaving no room for errors.



Cost Estimation Skills: Proficiency in cost estimation techniques, including knowledge of material and labor costs, as well as an understanding of market trends. Preparing accurate and competitive cost estimates for Commercial Painting (Division 09 – Finishes), Architectural Millwork (Division 06 – Wood, Plastics, and Composites) and Division 12 (Furnishings). Performing detailed quantity takeoffs across applicable CSI Divisions (01–49)



Organization and Time Management: Strong organizational skills to manage multiple projects simultaneously, meet deadlines, and prioritize tasks effectively.



Communication Skills: Excellent verbal and written communication skills to collaborate with project teams, vendors, and suppliers.



Analytical Thinking: Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities to identify and resolve issues during the takeoff process.

Ai: The industry is moving quickly – we want to embrace all the technology that will make us more efficient.



Why You’ll Love Working Here:



Supportive Team: Join a team that values collaboration and celebrates achievements.

Career Growth: Opportunities for professional development and advancement.

Loyalty: Tea
mbers who worked with the founder in a prior company have followed. Two have over 11 years with the founder. Generous opportunities for personal and professional development without the corporate garbage that steals your soul.



Health & Wellness Benefits: We care about your well-being and provide resources to keep you at your best.

Flexible Work Environment: Enjoy a balanced work-life with options for remote work.



Benefits:



80-120 Hours of PTO

7- Paid Holidays

13th Month Payroll

Paid Philippine HMO plan

Paid Social Security System

Paid Philippine Health

Paid training

Advancement after Proof of Mastery

Performance Goals with advancement compensation

Job Type: Full-time



Benefits:





Paid training

Work from home

Application Question(s):





Willing to work US hours (PH night shift)?

Willing to use a time tracking tool (Time Doctor) once hired?

Do you have a backup computer, backup Internet and dual monitors? (or willing to purchase missing requirements once hired)

Experience:





Remote/WFH: 2 years (Required)

US Construction Industry: 2 years (Required)

On-Screen Takeoff (OST) tool: 1 year (Required)

Work Location: Remote

title: Buyer / Purchaser
description:
We are seeking qualified Buyers/Purchasers based in or near Baliuag, Bulacan (3006). This is an on-site position requiring availability during U.S. business hours.





The Buyer sources, negotiates and buys goods, materials, and services to meet the operational

requirements, working with all departments and campuses to ensure timely deliveries and forecasts.

The individual sources items that meet the company’s price, quality, and delivery guidelines to

ensure continuity of operations.



? Prepare and issue purchase orders in line with specifications

? Respond with information or solutions to customer tickets in Zendesk, slack, or
 within

24 hours of inquiry

? Work with the operations team to ensure that all delivers satisfy the assigned purchase

orders and report any back-order/missing product

? Train new ordering secretaries, facility managers, or food service managers with proper

Workday procedures within employees' first 2 weeks



Continuous Improvement, Cost Savings:

? Issue requests for quotes/proposals and select the best source consistent with cost, quality,

and delivery requirements

? Negotiate and manage long-term pricing agreements on critical items

? Research and evaluate areas of opportunity and reduce costs where possible



Vendor Management

? Monitor supplier performance by ensuring that the product is delivered as scheduled and

meets specifications.

? Assist in vendor audits and evaluations

? Act as liaison between supplier and involved departments to resolve procurement-related

problems



Education, Certifications, and Experience

? 3+ years of related procurement experience, including purchasing, contract negotiation,

supplier relationships, and customer service

? Effective oral and written communication skills

? Proficiency with MS Office and Google Suite

title: Remote Appointment Setter
description:
We are seeking a highly motivated and reliable Virtual Assistant from the Philippines to join our fast-growing team as an Appointment Setter. Your role will be to engage with warm leads, build rapport, and book qualified appointments for our sales team. If you are hungry to grow, coachable, and results-driven, this is an incredible opportunity to earn great commissions and bonuses while working from home.

Responsibilities:

Reach out to prospects through messaging platforms,
, and follow-ups





Qualify leads and schedule appointments for our sales team





Maintain clear records of conversations and updates in our CRM as well as KPI Tracker





Meet daily and weekly booking targets





Collaborate with team leads to improve scripts and conversion rates





Requirements:

Excellent written and spoken English (near-native level)





Strong communication and interpersonal skills





Organized, detail-oriented, and consistent





Reliable internet connection and home office setup





Able to work during U.S. business hours





Compensation: Make up to $1750 a month! 

Base Salary: $250/month (paid $125 bi-weekly)



Commission: $50 per closed deal





Bonuses:



5 closes: $100 bonus



10 closes: $200 bonus



15 closes: $300 bonus



20 closes: $500 bonus





Example Earnings:

5 deals: $600/month total



10 deals: $950/month total



15 deals: $1,300/month total



20 deals: $1,750/month total





Payment Method: TransferWise or PayPal

Why Join Us:

Work from home and earn competitive pay in USD





Generous bonuses and commissions





Supportive and growth-focused team





Long-term career opportunities for high performers





How to Apply:

 Send us your application with:

1. A short introduction about yourself



2. Your relevant experience



3. Why you think you’d be a great fit for this role



 To prove you read this entire job post, please include your favorite color somewhere in your application.

We’re looking for someone who is eager to learn, self-motivated, and ready to grow with us. If that sounds like you, we want to hear from you!

title: Sales Agent (Remote) — InfiniMVP | Base Pay + $500–$1,000 Commission per Closed Deal
description:
About InfiniMVP

InfiniMVP builds MVPs fast (subscription-style) — web apps, dashboards, SaaS builds, prototypes — “concept to code” velocity. 



about-infinimvp



What you’ll do (daily/weekly)



Call warm + cold leads (LinkedIn, website inquiries, referrals)



Send cold
s + follow-ups (high volume, clean tracking)



Book qualified calls onto my calendar



Qualify prospects (budget, timeline, decision-maker, scope)



Maintain a pipeline (HubSpot or simple spreadsheet)



Help close deals (hand-off + assist with objections)



Commission (the big part)



$500–$1,000 per deal I close that you sourced/qualified (based on deal size + quality)



Base pay included (depends on experience + hours)



Who this is for



You’re comfortable calling people (no fear, no stalling)



You can write short, punchy outbound messages



You understand selling services (dev/agency/SaaS is a plus)



You’re organized and obsessed with follow-up



Requirements



Fluent English (spoken + written)



Reliable internet + quiet calling setup



Experience with cold calling/cold
 (show proof)

title: Commercial Property Operations Coordinator
description:

Job Overview:

We are seeking a detail-oriented and highly organized Commercial Property Operations Coordinator to support a commercial real estate portfolio of retail and office properties by overseeing maintenance tracking, vendor coordination, compliance monitoring, lease administration support, and day-to-day operational follow-ups. This role works closely with the onshore property management team to ensure tasks are properly tracked, vendors are consistently followed up with, timelines are met, and nothing falls through the cracks during high-volume periods. Key Responsibilities:

* Vendor Coordination & Follow-Up

Track incoming proposals and follow up on outstanding bids

Coordinate vendor scheduling and access after vendor selection

Collect close-out documentation (photos, completion confirmation, invoices)

Answer office phone

Track routine inspections, testing, and compliance items (fire/life safety, elevator, backflow, etc.)

Assist with COI tracking for tenants and vendors

Support documentation requests from tenants, vendors, lenders, and internal team

Maintain organized digital files and trackers for commercial properties

* Lease & Property Administration (Non-Legal)

Maintain lease abstract summaries (critical dates, options, notice deadlines)

Track upcoming renewals, options, and notice requirements

Prepare routine notices using approved templates for review

Maintain updated tenant contact lists and
 distribution lists.



* Task Tracking & Operational Support

Provide weekly status summaries of open items and priorities

Familiarity with maintenance workflows, vendor coordination, and property documentation

Strong organizational and follow-up skills

Excellent written English and professional communication skills

Comfortable working with spreadsheets, trackers, and task management tools

Detail-oriented, reliable, and comfortable working behind the scenes

Able to manage multiple tasks and follow up consistently without dropping items



Note: An NBI or police clearance will be required upon acceptance of the job offer.

title: Bookkeeper / Accountant Pacific Time Zone
description:
Job Title: Full-Time Bookkeeper / Accountant ( Pacific Time Zone Availability Required)



Company Overview:



We are a US-landscape company with real estate holdings. 



We are seeking a dedicated, experienced Filipino virtual professional to fully own the bookkeeping across these entities. 



This full-time role combines hands-on bookkeeping with accountant-level oversight and CFO-minded strategic support.



You will ensure books are accurate, CPA-ready, and provide actionable financial insights for decision-making. The role requires strong independence, professional judgment, and full accountability. Prior experience with US-based or multi-entity businesses is highly valued.



Important: Availability during Pacific Time Zone (PST/PDT) business hours is required for real-time collaboration, follow-ups, and alignment with US operations.



Key Responsibilities:



Daily and Operational Accounting:



Enter and code receipts, bills, checks, debits, and credits into QuickBooks Desktop (QBD) and Aspire (once implemented), with correct class, customer, and job classifications.

Perform daily reconciliations with vendor bills, statements, bank/credit card accounts for accuracy.

Manage full Accounts Payable (A/P) and Accounts Receivable (A/R): run reports, process payments per SOPs, handle credit memos and applications.

Organize and file receipts digitally.

Follow up promptly with personnel on incomplete or uncoded items.

Review, post, and reconcile bank/credit card transactions daily and monthly.

Manage clearing accounts, timing differences, owner distributions, and equity transactions.



Month-End and Financial Close:



Prepare and post journal entries (accruals, depreciation, reclassifications, corrections, equity adjustments).

Generate, review, and stand behind monthly financial statements (Profit & Loss, Balance Sheet) – clean, consistent, and defensible.

Close books accurately within two weeks of month-end.

Maintain fixed asset and depreciation schedules per CPA.

Proactively identify, investigate, and correct errors or misclassifications.



Compliance, Controls, and Tax Support:



Implement and enforce robust accounting controls and review processes.

Keep books audit-ready, consistent month-over-month, and fully prepared for CPA/tax reviews.

Prepare data for tax compliance (sales tax, payroll tax, etc.) and support CPA with annual filings, year-end reviews, or inquiries.

Flag risks, inconsistencies, or structural issues early.



Strategic Financial Oversight and Advisory:



Oversee budgeting, forecasting, and tracking systems (including Google Sheets controls).

Reconcile QuickBooks with external tools (budget sheets, CRM, etc.) monthly and explain variances.

Deliver insights on cash flow, cost control, and operational decisions.

Recommend process, reporting, or structural improvements as businesses grow.

Act as go-to expert for financial questions, making informed judgment calls and explaining impacts clearly.



Additional:



Support smooth transition to Aspire software.

Collaborate across entities, prioritizing GLAD while giving equal attention to real estate.



Who We're Looking For:



A seasoned professional who is not just a bookkeeper, but thinks like an Accountant/CFO—not just a task executor. You own the financial function end-to-end with confidence.



Must-Have Qualifications:



Proven Accounting Manager with full book ownership.

Extensive hands-on QuickBooks Desktop expertise (reconciliations, journal entries, A/P-A/R, equity, CPA-ready financials).

Proficiency with Google Sheets for controls/reporting.

Experience in service-based, real estate, or multi-entity environments preferred.

Strong independent judgment, accountability, and proactive mindset.

Excellent English (written/verbal) for clear communication.

Philippines-based with reliable internet and back-up computers/generators



Preferred:



Familiarity with Aspire or similar industry software.

US client or multi-business experience.



Time Commitment and Compensation:



Full-time- Must align with Pacific Time Zone business hours for meetings, follow-ups, and real-time collaboration—typically 8 AM–5 PM PST/PDT availability required).

Outcomes-focused: accuracy, ownership, and timeliness 

Long-term role with growth potential as businesses expand.

Competitive monthly salary in PHP (please state your expected rate in your application).



What's Important to Us:



Accuracy + efficiency.

True ownership and proactive accountability.

Independent operation with sound judgment.

Month-to-month consistency.

Professional, clear communication.



How to Apply:



To show attention to detail, follow this format exactly. 



Subject Line: Full-Time Virtual Accountant / Controller Application – Your Full Name



 Body / Application (include all):



Brief video introduction (2–3 minutes max) covering:

Your Bookkeeper/Accountant/CFO-level experience.

Years hands-on with QuickBooks Desktop.

Types of companies/businesses you've managed finances for.

Resume (PDF).

Cover letter: Why you're ideal for this combined role, confirmation of Pacific Time Zone availability, and your expected monthly salary in PHP.



We look forward to hearing from strong candidates ready to own this important function!

title: Senior Electrical Engineer – MEP & Energy Calculations
description:
We are seeking a Senior Electrical Engineer to lead electrical design and calculations for complex architectural and MEP projects. This is a remote position, supporting US-based projects in the EST timezone.

Review and validate MEP calculations from internal teams and external consultants.



Collaborate with architectural, mechanical, and plumbing teams to coordinate multi-discipline designs.



Ensure Life Safety, Means of Egress, and ADA compliance for electrical systems.



Contribute to permit-ready Construction Document (CD) sets and ensure adherence to Miami / Florida Building Codes.



Review technical specifications, quantity takeoffs, and CSI documentation for accuracy.



Estimate project hours and prepare detailed scopes of work.



Lead and mentor remote production teams (minimum 2 years of people management experience required).



Support energy modeling, performance compliance, and sustainability documentation.

5+ years in Electrical Engineering within AEC, construction, or MEP environments.



Proven experience producing and reviewing permit-ready CD sets.



Strong knowledge of Miami / Florida Building Codes.



Experience coordinating across architectural and MEP disciplines.



2+ years managing engineering or production teams.



US-based project experience preferred.



Proficiency in BIM, Revit, AutoCAD, and construction documentation workflows.



Preferred Qualifications:



Licensed Professional Engineer (PE) in Electrical Engineering.



Experience supporting US permitting workflows.



Experience with remote engineering production environments.



Work Setup:



Fully remote



EST timezone availability required



We are looking for a structured, detail-oriented technical leader who can deliver accurate calculations, permit-ready designs, and cross-disciplinary coordination under Florida code requirements. If you are an experienced electrical engineer with leadership experience and a track record of high-quality engineering deliverables, we want to connect with you.

 / message subject line: POD EDITOR – [Your Name]

nts from the episode to hook the viewer.

title: Podcast Video Editor
description:
About the Role

We run a video podcast and need a project-based editor to turn raw Riverside recordings into clean, engaging, YouTube-ready episodes and occasional short clips.



This is paid per episode / per project, with the option for ongoing work if you’re good, fast, and reliable.



We care about:

1. Clean, professional edits

2. Good pacing and flow

3. Reliability and speed



You’ll work with a small, fast-moving team and grow with the show long?term.



Responsibilities:

1. Edit full podcast episodes from Riverside (multi-track).

2. Clean up audio (noise reduction, EQ, leveling).

3. Remove dead time, long pauses, obvious tangents, and major ums/ahs.

4. Add basic graphics:

- Lower thirds (names / topics)

- Simple transitions

5. Do basic color correction for a consistent look.

6. Export and organize final files in our shared drive with clear naming.

7. Occasionally cut 30–90 second clips from episodes for social.



Requirements

1. Proven experience editing podcast / talking-head style videos.

2. Comfortable with multi-track recordings (Riverside, Zoom, etc.).

3.  Reliable internet and able to hit deadlines.



Pay & Scope

1. Project-based: paid per edited episode (we’ll agree on a rate based on your skill).

2. Potential for recurring projects if quality and communication are strong.

3. Payment via Deel



How to Apply (Instructions – read carefully)

1.
 / message subject line: POD EDITOR – [Your Name]

- Send to 

2. Include ALL of the following in your first message:

a. Links to your work

- 2-5 samples of your works.

b. Short answer (2–3 sentences):

- What makes a podcast edit feel professional and engaging?

Your rate & availability

c. Rate per full episode (in USD) for:

- 45–90 min raw ? cleaned, edited YouTube episode.

- Your average turnaround time per episode

d. REQUIRED TEST(DO THIS NOW)

Complete this test and include the link to your export in your application:



Download the raw Riverside recording here:





Your task:

1. Edit a 2-minute intro to the episode, inspired by Diary of a CEO style.

2. Use the most emotional / impactful 
nts from the episode to hook the viewer.

3. Use jump cuts and pacing to keep it engaging.

4. Add subtle background music that fits the tone.

5. Keep graphics minimal and clean.



Deliverables:

1. 1x 16:9 horizontal video, max 2:00, 1080p MP4.

2. Upload to Google Drive (or similar) and include a viewable link in your application.



Applications without the completed test edit will not be considered.

title: Surplus Funds VA/LeadGen
description:
We are looking for a detail-oriented virtual assistant to help identify and research potential surplus fund opportunities from foreclosure and tax sale cases across multiple counties in the United States.



Your role will involve reviewing public court records, identifying cases where excess proceeds exist after foreclosure sales, and compiling organized lead lists for outreach.



This position is ideal for someone who enjoys research, working with public records, maintaining organized data, and ideally has some knowledge in surplus funds.



Responsibilities



• Review foreclosure and sheriff sale records from county court websites

• Identify cases where excess proceeds or surplus funds may exist

• Extract and record case details, including property address, case number, sale price, and surplus amount

• Research property owners and possible heirs using public records

• Perform basic skip tracing to locate contact information (phone numbers,
s, addresses) (training in systems will be taught)

• Maintain organized lead lists in spreadsheets or CRM

• Assist with updating and tracking outreach campaigns

• Ensure all collected information is accurate and well-documented



Required Skills



• Strong attention to detail

• Ability to navigate county court websites and public record databases

• Experience with lead generation or data research

• Comfortable working with spreadsheets (Google Sheets / Excel)

• Strong written English communication

• Ability to follow step-by-step research procedures



Preferred Experience (Not Required)



• Real estate research

• Foreclosure or tax sale knowledge

• Skip tracing experience

• CRM tools (non-specific, just experience in navigating)

• Data scraping or web research

title: PPC Specialist for Plumbing / Home Services Industry
description:
We are seeking a highly skilled PPC Specialist with proven expertise in both Bing Ads / Meta / and Google Ads. The PPC Specialist will be responsible for planning, executing, and optimizing paid search campaigns focused on generating qualified inbound calls and form submissions for plumbing services.



Plan, launch, and manage PPC campaigns across Bing / Ads and Google Ads focused on lead generation.

Conduct keyword research, audience targeting, and competitor analysis to identify new growth opportunities.

Write, test, and refine ad copy to maximize CTR and conversion rates.



Optimize campaigns daily for CPL, CPA, ROAS, and lead quality.

Set up and manage conversion tracking, UTMs, and analytics integrations.

Perform A/B testing on ad creatives, bidding strategies, and landing pages.

Monitor campaign performance and provide weekly and monthly performance reports.

Identify trends, insights, and areas for improvement to scale performance effectively.

Collaborate with the management and operations teams to align lead flow with service capacity.

Stay current with industry trends, Google and Microsoft Ads updates, and emerging PPC strategies.



Requirements

Minimum 2+ years of hands-on PPC experience (Google / Meta and Bing Ads required).

Proven success in lead generation campaigns (preferably in service-based industries).

Strong proficiency in Google Ads, Microsoft Advertising, Google Analytics, and Tag Manager.

Deep understanding of bidding strategies, keyword optimization, and conversion tracking.

Experience with landing page optimization and data analysis.

Excellent English communication skills, both written and verbal.

Strong analytical, problem-solving, and time management skills.

Must be able to work during PST business hours.



Benefits

This is a full-time remote position, requiring a stable internet connection and a quiet workspace.

Must be available to work shifts aligned with Pacific Standard Time (PST).

Compensation is provided weekly.

Can start immediately.



Proven experience managing operational or cross-functional projects.

Exceptional Organization: Strong coordination skills with the ability to manage shifting priorities.

Communication: Crystal-clear written and verbal communication skills.

Process-Driven: Experience documenting processes or building internal workflows.

Analytical Thinking: Ability to translate information into structured action plans.



How to Apply (Required Step)

To ensure your application is processed through our internal hiring system, please complete your application by sending your Resume and Cover Letter to:


 (remove space)



Subject Line: Project Manager Application - [Your Full Name]

Note: Only applications sent to the
 address above will be moved to the interview stage if shortlisted.
