title: Jr Checkout Champ Developer
description:
Learn from the best. Build funnels that print revenue.



Department:  IT/ Production

Type:  Full-Time | Remote

Reports To:  Senior Checkout Champ Developer

Compensation:  Competitive Base 

Location:  Worldwide | Remote



ABOUT AJ MEDIA

AJ Media is a performance-driven ecommerce growth agency scaling consumer brands toward $100M. Our engineering team builds the checkout systems, upsell flows, and funnel logic that convert traffic into revenue. We don't build for aesthetics — we build for conversion rate, average order value, and repeatability. Every line of code has a business outcome attached to it.



THE ROLE

This is a junior role — but it is NOT a passive one. You will work directly alongside our Senior Checkout Champ Developer, get hands-on with real funnel builds from day one, and be expected to grow into independent ownership within 6–12 months. If you're looking for a role where you watch and wait — this isn't it.

Our senior developer owns the full Checkout Champ and Shopify funnel stack. You will support and learn from them — implementing upsells, debugging checkout flows, assisting with Shopify customizations, and gradually taking on builds of your own. You bring foundational Checkout Champ and Shopify knowledge. We'll give you the environment to level up fast.



HOW THIS ROLE DIFFERS FROM THE SENIOR POSITION

The Senior Developer owns architecture and leads all complex builds. The Junior Developer executes components of those builds, owns smaller scoped tasks independently, troubleshoots under guidance, and builds toward full ownership. If you're applying to grow into the senior role — this is the path.



What the Senior Developer owns:

End-to-end Checkout Champ funnel architecture and strategy

Custom subscription flows and complex rebill logic

Full API integrations between platforms from spec to deployment

Payment gateway configuration and advanced troubleshooting

Final QA sign-off and production deploys



What you'll own and grow into:

Implementing upsell and downsell sequences built to the senior developer's spec

Shopify theme customizations and Liquid template edits

Tracking and pixel setup support (UTM structures, conversion events)

Checkout flow testing, bug documentation, and first-level troubleshooting

Building and maintaining internal documentation for funnel components

Taking on end-to-end ownership of smaller builds as your skill set develops



WHAT YOU'LL DO

Checkout Champ:

Assist in building and configuring Checkout Champ funnels under senior developer direction

Implement upsell and downsell offers, order bumps, and post-purchase sequences

Support payment gateway configuration and help troubleshoot checkout flow errors

Test checkout flows across devices, browsers, and payment methods — document all findings

Assist with subscription and rebill logic setup as you build familiarity with the platform



Shopify:

Customize Shopify themes using Liquid, HTML, and CSS

Implement front-end changes based on design briefs — product pages, landing pages, and checkout

Debug display or functional issues across storefronts for Gekko Gifts, Purvos, and Besyner

Support the senior developer on API integration work and third-party app configuration



Tracking & Performance Support:

Assist with pixel and tracking setup — Meta, Google, and native platform events

Help identify and document tracking discrepancies — work with the team to resolve

Understand the relationship between checkout flow changes and conversion metrics



Learning & Documentation:

Document builds, component logic, and processes as you execute them

Proactively review Checkout Champ and Shopify release notes and flag relevant changes

Ask good questions — and translate what you learn into reusable references for the team



WHO YOU ARE

1–2 years of hands-on experience with Checkout Champ — you've built or assisted with at least one live funnel

Solid Shopify fundamentals — comfortable with Liquid, theme customization, and Shopify admin

Working knowledge of JavaScript and basic front-end development (HTML, CSS)

You understand what a conversion funnel is — and why every step matters

Detail-oriented: you test your own work before marking it done, and you document what you build

You communicate clearly in writing — async-first team, remote environment, clarity is non-negotiable

Hungry to learn: you read documentation, watch build videos, and test things on your own time

Not precious about feedback — you want to know what's wrong so you can fix it



PORTFOLIO REQUIREMENT

Portfolio is mandatory. Applications without Checkout Champ or Shopify project examples will not be reviewed. Junior does not mean no evidence — it means less volume. Show us what you've built, even if it's one solid funnel.



We're looking for:

At least one Checkout Champ funnel you worked on — screenshot, Loom walkthrough, or live link

At least one Shopify project — theme customization, a landing page, or a checkout modification

If you've built less: show us what you know. A short video walkthrough of your strongest project tells us more than a CV.



STRONG PLUS

Experience with post-purchase upsell apps (ReConvert, Zipify, or similar)

Familiarity with subscription platforms (Recharge, Bold Subscriptions, or Checkout Champ rebill flows)

Exposure to media buying workflows — you understand what a media buyer cares about from the funnel

Basic understanding of conversion rate optimization principles

Experience with any API integration work, even simple webhook or Zapier flows



HIRING PROCESS

Submit portfolio (required — no exceptions)

Short take-home technical test (Checkout Champ + Shopify task — 2–3 hours)

Final interview with Senior Developer and team lead

This is a long-term opportunity. We are building a conversion engineering team that will scale with the business. The right junior developer will have a clear path to Senior Developer within 12–18 months based on output quality and ownership growth.



WHY JOIN US

Work directly under a senior developer with deep Checkout Champ expertise — one of the best learning environments available for this stack

See your code go live on real, high-traffic ecommerce brands — not staging environments

Performance bonus tied to funnel output quality and conversion improvements

Fast growth path to Senior Developer for candidates who execute and take initiative

Remote-first, results-focused team — you are measured on what you ship, not your hours

Work across three growing consumer brands with more launches planned



Ready to apply?  Submit your portfolio link and a short note on the most complex Checkout Champ or Shopify task you've completed. Walk us through what it was, what you built, and what you'd do differently now. We read every application personally.

title: Bookkeeper – Australian Accountants Office – Work from home
description:
Bookkeeper – Australian Accountants Office – Work from home - 2 Positions available



We are seeking an experienced bookkeeper based in the Philippines who has real, hands-on experience with Australian GST.



This role is for someone who understands why transactions are treated a certain way under Australian tax rules, not just how to enter data into accounting software.



ABOUT US

Cascade Accounting & Tax is an Australian accounting practice supporting small businesses and professional firms across Australia. We already have four happy Philippines based tea
mbers who have been with us between 13 months and 3 years.

We are a fully paperless, systems-driven firm. Accuracy, documentation, and sound accounting judgement are essential in our work.



THIS ROLE IS A GOOD FIT IF YOU:

• Have practical experience working with Australian GST

• Understand the difference between cash and accrual accounting for GST

• Know why a BAS may not match the GST control account at a point in time

• Can correctly identify GST-free, input-taxed, and mixed-use transactions

• Are comfortable asking questions before coding transactions

• Follow naming conventions and file source documents carefully

• Prefer doing work correctly rather than quickly



THIS ROLE IS NOT SUITABLE IF YOU:

• Only process data in Xero or QuickBooks without understanding the results

• Use suspense accounts to force balances to reconcile

• Guess GST treatment when unsure

• Avoid documenting your work or explaining adjustments

• Expect full independence without following established procedures

• Want to re-invent our effective work procedures



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

• End-to-end bookkeeping for Australian clients

• Accurate GST coding and review

• Bank and credit card reconciliations

• Preparation of BAS data

• Identifying and escalating issues clearly

• Maintaining clean, well-structured source documents and workpapers

• Working within documented systems and workflows



SOFTWARE EXPERIENCE

• QuickBooks Online

• Xero

(Software skills are important. Australian GST knowledge is essential.)



WHAT WE VALUE



• Strong accounting fundamentals

• Attention to detail

• Clear written communication

• Professional judgement

• Willingness to say “I’m not sure, I need to check”



APPLICATION INSTRUCTIONS (PLEASE READ CAREFULLY)



To apply, please
 
 and please include:

1. A short cover message written by you, explaining:

o When you discovered you liked bookkeeping

o Your experience working with Australian GST

o What would you do if an account on a clients’ Balance Sheet showed a significantly different balance to what you expected.

o One common GST mistake you see in Australian client files

2. Your Curriculum Vitae (different to a one-page marketing brochure)

Shortlisted applicants will complete a practical fundamentals of bookkeeping assessment, on zoom with one of our team before being offered and interview.

We assess how you think, not how polished your application looks.



LOCATION & HOURS

• Remote role – work from home

• Ongoing full-time work

• Hours and pay discussed with suitable candidates



ESSENTIAL EQUIPMENT

• PC or Laptop minimum i5 processor; minimum 8 mb RAM (preferably 16 mb)

• Reliable fast internet and backup internet

• Licenced copy of Microsoft Office with operational copies of Word and Excel on your desktop

• At least 2 external monitors of 24 inch or larger

• External keyboard with number pad and mouse

• Noise cancelling headphones with microphone for tea
etings

• Ergonomic desk and chair in quiet location away from other family members

• Extra Adult in home to mind any children under 8 years old during working hours
salary: AUD $7.50 per hour to AUD $15 per hour

title: SEO Specialist
description:
We are looking for a skilled SEO specialist to help organize and grow the organic traffic of a large knowledge website. The role focuses on building strong site architecture, creating topical clusters, improving internal linking, and developing backlink strategies to increase authority.



This is a remote role that might require morning meetings some days during US Eastern Time mornings (until 10 AM ET).



Must skill: SEO Architecture & Content Clustering & Backlink Building, WordPress, Google Search Console



Nice to have: Using LLMs for Strategy, Other Seo Tools



Your application
 subject line MUST read:



"I actually read the SEO instructions"



Applications with any other subject line will not be reviewed.



Who We Are



We are Buzz Insider, working with B2B companies across multiple industries. Our focus is building scalable content platforms that generate long-term organic growth.



We help companies transform large content libraries into structured authority websites through strong architecture, topical clusters, internal linking, and high-quality backlinks.



Our leadership team has very high expectations and values people who go the extra mile with creative problem-solving approaches.



 www.buzzinsider.co



Salary & Hours



Starting at $5–10/hour depending on experience, with increases based on performance.



Initial work will likely be a 40-80 hour project on one website over a few weeks and than about 10 hours per week for ongoing, with potential to scale as project demands grow.



Core Responsibilities



Analyze large content websites (2000+ articles) and design SEO-driven site architecture.



Build topical clusters and pillar page structures to organize large content libraries.



Identify articles targeting similar keywords and restructure or consolidate them.



Design and implement internal linking frameworks that strengthen topical authority.



Create cluster maps connecting pillar pages with supporting articles.



Develop blog content plans based on keyword clusters.



Support new blog creation and content expansion.



Identify keyword opportunities for ranking and set up AI content systems to produce



Plan and execute backlink building strategies to increase domain authority.



Identify and pursue opportunities for:



guest posting



industry partnerships



resource page links



editorial mentions



Assist with link outreach campaigns and track backlink performance.



Document SEO structure and maintain clear records of changes.



Key Requirements



Strong experience building SEO architecture for large content websites.



Deep understanding of:



topical authority



pillar pages



content clusters



internal linking structures



Experience organizing hundreds or thousands of articles into structured hierarchies.



Experience with backlink building strategies and outreach.



Experience using SEO tools such as:



Google Search Console



Screaming Frog



Ahrefs or SEMrush



Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.



Candidate Profile



You are methodical, analytical, and structured. You enjoy organizing complex content ecosystems and building scalable SEO systems.



Traits we value:



Logical thinker

Detail-oriented

Self-driven

Strong problem solver

Focused on long-term growth



What energizes you:



Building topical authority websites

Structuring large content libraries

Growing traffic through architecture and backlinks

Creating SEO systems that scale



SEO Case Study Request



Please include one real SEO project you worked on and explain:



The website URL



The main SEO problem



The actions you implemented



The result (traffic, rankings, or impressions)



Example format:



Website:





Problem:

Large content site with weak architecture and low authority.



Actions Taken:

Reorganized articles into topical clusters, created pillar pages, improved internal linking, and implemented backlink outreach.



Result:

Organic traffic increased from 12K/month to 48K/month over 8 months.



If possible, include a screenshot from Google Search Console, Ahrefs, or SEMrush showing the improvement.



To Apply



Send your application to:







Only
s sent to this address will be considered.



Your submission must include:



Resume (PDF link, format: 
O.pdf)



Seo Case Study



2–3 websites you worked on and what SEO work you performed. Include the impact on traffic or rankings.



How would you build pillars and topical clusters and let Google know or signal it about the information architecture of the site.

title: SMS Sales Assistant – High-Growth Wedding Studio ($5/hr + Bonuses)
description:
ABOUT THE COMPANY

We are a leading U.S. multi-brand wedding photography and videography studio. We are 100% remote and currently booking 30+ weddings per month. We are looking for a disciplined, fast-moving professional to join our team and help us scale that number through rapid SMS engagement and consistent follow-up. This is a long-term position.



ROLE SUMMARY

You will join a 3-person SMS Concierge Team handling inbound leads via text inside GoHighLevel (GHL). There are no voice calls required. Your goal is to qualify leads, nurture them via SMS, and "warm hand-off" prospects to a U.S. closer for a scheduled call.



COMPENSATION



Base Rate: $5.00 USD per hour (Tracked via TimeProof).



Booking Bonus: $10.00 USD per tea
mber for every wedding booked.



Total Monthly Potential (Estimated):



30 bookings/month = $300 bonus + ~$700 base = $1,000+ USD/month



50 bookings/month = $500 bonus + ~$700 base = $1,200+ USD/month



WHAT YOU’LL DO



Speed-to-Lead: Reply to new inbound leads instantly inside GHL.



Nurture: Run structured SMS follow-ups using our proven scripts.



Schedule: Book calendar calls for our sales team and provide a clean lead summary.



Data Integrity: Maintain accurate notes, tags, and pipeline statuses in GHL.



Drafting: Use our shared ChatGPT account to help polish and personalize responses.



SCHEDULE & LOGISTICS



Hours: 35 hours per week.



Coverage: The team covers 8:00 AM – 10:00 PM EST, 7 days a week (scheduled in shifts).



Location: 100% Work from home.



YOU ARE A FIT IF:



You type fast and write with clear, natural, U.S.-style English.



You thrive in a metrics-driven, bonus-heavy environment.



You are highly organized and can strictly follow checklists and response-time SLAs.



You have a stable internet connection and a reliable computer.



Nice-to-have: Experience with GoHighLevel (GHL), appointment setting, or the wedding/hospitality industry.



WHAT WE PROVIDE



Full paid training, scripts, and objection-handling guides.



Clear KPIs and weekly feedback sessions.



On-time payments through OnlineJobs.ph approved methods.



HOW TO APPLY (FOLLOW EXACTLY)

Please respond via OLJ with the following information:



A link to your recent CV/Resume.



A 3–5 sentence summary of your SMS/chat-based sales or support experience.



A link to a recent internet speed test and your computer specifications.



Your typical working hours (in US - EST) and your weekly availability.



Confirm that you are comfortable using TimeProof, communicating in Slack, and working as part of a coordinated 3-person team.



We are hiring three people to operate as one coordinated unit. Training starts ASAP!

Send approved
s and texts related to:

Quote readiness



Appointment confirmations

title: Operations Automation Specialist (API Integrations / Workflow Automation)
description:
Job Description



We are looking for an automation specialist to help streamline several operational workflows across our systems. The goal is to reduce manual tasks by connecting systems through APIs and automation platforms.



This role focuses on building practical, maintainable automations, not complex infrastructure or large backend systems.



Many workflows involve pulling data from APIs, transforming the data, and updating structured records such as Google Sheets or internal reports.



Experience with workflow automation platforms such as 
, Zapier, n8n, or Microsoft Power Automate is preferred.



Example Automations We Want to Build



Typical workflows may include:



• Pulling data from APIs (inventory, orders, reports)

• Transforming and normalizing data (SKU, quantities, pricing, etc.)

• Updating Google Sheets or internal reporting tables

• Comparing data between systems and identifying mismatches

• Automating report consolidation from multiple sources

• Running scheduled workflows (daily or hourly)

• Sending alerts when validation rules fail



Example workflow:



API ? Data transformation ? Google Sheets update ? validation ? alert if mismatch



Requirements



We are looking for someone with experience in:



• Workflow automation platforms (
, Zapier, n8n, Power Automate)

• REST API integrations

• JSON / CSV data parsing

• Handling API pagination and rate limits

• Google Sheets integrations

• Error handling and retry logic

• Preventing duplicate records when writing data

• Building clean and maintainable automation workflows



Nice to Have



Experience integrating with systems such as:



• Shopify

• Amazon Seller APIs

• ERP or inventory systems

• Accounting platforms



Important Notes



We are not looking to build large backend systems or complex infrastructure. The goal is to keep automations lean, reliable, and easy to maintain using appropriate automation tools.



To Apply, Please Answer



Describe an automation workflow you built that involved pulling data from an API and writing it to another system.



How do you handle API pagination when retrieving large datasets?



How do you prevent duplicate records when writing to Google Sheets or another destination?



How do you log errors and retry failed operations in your automation workflows?



Which automation platforms or tools do you have the most experience with?

title: LinkedIn Researcher & Outreach Coordinator
description:
About the Role

You will be the human intelligence layer across four companies: a defence advisory firm, a healthcare compliance platform, a technology development company, and a creative agency. Each business targets senior decision-makers (CEOs, MDs, Founders, Partners) at a specific type of company, and your job is to find the right people, reach them, and manage the conversation until a meeting is booked.



You will work alongside an existing technical VA who handles automated research via Claude Code and Airtable. Your role complements his. He generates volume through programmatic search. You do what automation cannot: verify profiles manually, find the people LinkedIn blocks from scraping, judge whether someone fits the archetype, send personalised outreach, and manage responses through to a booked meeting.



You will use the company’s LinkedIn Recruiter account as your primary research tool.



What You Will Actually Do



Profile Research & Verification

Use LinkedIn Recruiter to find senior profiles matching specific archetypes for each company (e.g. retired Colonels running independent defence consultancies, independent pharmacy owners, CEOs of mid-market tech companies, CHROs at £1bn+ businesses)

Verify every profile before submission. If a profile is not a real person at the right type of company, do not include it. Accuracy is non-negotiable

Cross-reference automated lists produced by the technical VA, flagging fabricated or mismatched profiles

Deliver clean lists in a four-column format: Full Name, LinkedIn URL, Company, Role



Outreach & Response Management

Send personalised LinkedIn connection requests and follow-up messages on behalf of the founder, using approved messaging templates adapted to each recipient

Monitor responses across all four companies daily

When a prospect responds positively: obtain their
 address, create their record in Airtable, and notify the PA (Grace) to schedule a meeting

When a prospect delegates (e.g. “Speak to my HR Director”): draft and send the appropriate follow-up
 to the referred contact, then track that thread through to a meeting

When a prospect declines or goes cold: log the outcome in Airtable and move on



Pipeline Hygiene

Keep Airtable updated with every contact, interaction, and status change

Ensure no lead falls through the cracks across four simultaneous company pipelines

Flag patterns you notice (e.g. a particular archetype converting well, a messaging angle getting better response rates)



Requirements



Non-Negotiable

Proven experience with LinkedIn Sales Navigator or LinkedIn Recruiter. You must be able to build Boolean searches, use filters effectively, and navigate the platform without training

Extreme diligence. You will be managing outreach across four companies simultaneously. Sending the wrong message to the wrong person, or mixing up which company a prospect belongs to, is a serious failure. If you are someone who makes careless mistakes under pressure, this role is not for you

Strong written English. You will draft
s and LinkedIn messages on behalf of a senior entrepreneur. The tone must be warm, direct, and professional. Not salesy, not stiff

Comfort with Airtable or willingness to learn it quickly. It is the central system for tracking everything



Preferred

Background in B2B lead generation, executive recruiting, or business development research

Experience working with remote founders or executives across multiple time zones

Familiarity with CRM systems and pipeline management



How You Will Be Assessed

After an initial screening, shortlisted candidates will complete a paid trial day. You will be given access to LinkedIn Recruiter and a brief describing the target archetype for each company. You will have the full day to produce verified profile lists and demonstrate your research methodology.

You will be judged on accuracy first, volume second. Every profile must be a real, verified person at the right type of company. If you find 25 perfect profiles, submit 25. Do not pad to 50 with unverified ones.



Working Arrangement

Remote, working UK business hours (9am–6pm GMT/BST)

Weekly payment initially, moving to monthly once the working relationship is established

Rate: competitive for the Philippines market, discussed at interview



How to Apply

Send a short message explaining your LinkedIn research experience, including which LinkedIn tools you have used (Sales Navigator, Recruiter, Recruiter Lite), how many years you have used them, and one example of a research or lead generation project you completed. Include your LinkedIn profile URL.



To confirm you have read this entire job description, begin your application with the word “Verified” as the first word of your message. Applications that do not begin with this word will not be reviewed.



Do not send a generic cover letter. I will not read it.

What You’ll Do (Core Responsibilities)



1) WordPress / Bricks Builder (Primary)

Build and edit sections/pages in Bricks Builder

Improve layout, spacing, responsiveness (desktop/mobile)

Apply basic CSS when needed (small fixes, styling tweaks)

Upload and format new pages and blog posts

Add/replace images and keep pages visually clean



2) On-Page SEO (Rank Math)

Edit SEO titles + meta descriptions

Ensure correct heading structure (H1–H3)

Add internal links (based on instructions/SOPs)

Add schema using Rank Math when instructed

Follow templates/SOPs we provide (no strategy required)





What We Need (Requirements)



Strong WordPress publishing skills and attention to detail

Bricks Builder experience (not just Elementor/Divi)

Comfortable with basic CSS (small edits and troubleshooting)

Basic on-page SEO understanding (titles, metas, headers, internal links)

Can follow SOPs and self-manage tasks without constant direction

Reliable internet + consistent availability



Nice to Have (Bonus)

Canva / basic graphic editing

Local SEO familiarity 



How We Work

You’ll receive tasks + SOPs, and you’re also expected to create your own tasks when you see obvious improvements (layout fixes, formatting cleanups, internal linking opportunities) and report them.



Expectation: clean execution, good communication, and weekly progress updates.



To Apply (READ CAREFULLY)

Put “BRICKS” as the first word in your application, then answer all questions below. Applications missing answers will be ignored.



How many years have you worked with WordPress?

How long have you used Bricks Builder? What types of pages/sections did you build?

Have you used Rank Math or Yoast? What exact tasks did you perform?

Google Business Profile experience: Yes/No + 2–3 tasks you’ve done

What is your expected monthly salary in PHP?

What is your internet speed? (Upload a screenshot of a speed test)

What is your typical working schedule/timezone?



Optional (highly preferred): Record a short Loom (2–4 mins) showing a Bricks page you edited and explain what you changed.

title: Finance and Accounting Officer
description:
We're Hiring: Hospitality Finance Director (CFO-Level Role)



(SEND ME A
 WITH YOUR VIDEO RECORDING AND RESUME/CV, I will not be reading the inbox here) 



We are seeking a highly skilled and experienced Hospitality Finance Director to join our Airbnb management business as our Chief Financial Officer (CFO). This leadership role requires exceptional financial expertise, strong analytical skills, and the confidence to make high-level strategic decisions. You will oversee financial operations, provide data-driven insights, and ensure the overall financial health and scalability of the business. Flexibility, accountability, and a willingness to work occasional weekends are required.



IMPORTANT NOTE:

We are not looking for just an accountant or someone with basic bookkeeping skills. We already have an accountant who handles taxes and general bookkeeping. We need a true CFO — a strategic financial leader who can help us grow, scale, and build a high-performing financial infrastructure.



Key Responsibilities



Financial Oversight

 - Manage all financial operations of the company, including income and expense tracking

 - Build and maintain detailed budgets and forecasts aligned with business goals

 - Analyze financial performance, trends, and variances to uncover opportunities for improvement



Financial Reporting & Bookkeeping

 - Prepare and maintain daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, and annual financial reports

 - Compile and organize all financial information using QuickBooks and Google Sheets, ensuring data is accurate, timely, and actionable

 - Oversee internal bookkeeping processes: manage transactions, categorize expenses, reconcile accounts, and generate reports for decision-making

 - Collaborate with our third-party accounting firm to ensure compliance and accurate financial records



Payroll & Labor Cost Management

 - Oversee payroll processing and ensure timely, accurate payments to staff

 - Track labor costs and provide recommendations for cost efficiency

 

Cost Tracking & Internal Controls

 - Implement and maintain robust internal controls to monitor operational and departmental expenses

 - Monitor expenditures and ensure alignment with approved budgets and forecasts

 

Strategic Financial Leadership

 - Provide financial insights and recommendations to the executive team

 - Participate in strategic planning and business growth initiatives

 - Confidently challenge leadership decisions based on financial data and performance metrics



Regulatory Compliance & Audit Readiness

 - Ensure compliance with tax requirements, financial regulations, and industry standards

 - Prepare documentation for audits, financial reviews, and investor reports



Key Qualifications

 - Minimum 5+ years of experience in financial leadership roles (hospitality, real estate, or Airbnb experience preferred)

 - Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or a related field (CPA or CFA is a plus)

 - Strong command of QuickBooks, Google Sheets, and other financial tools

 - Experience managing payroll systems and preparing performance-based financial reports



Soft Skills We Value

 - Strong analytical and problem-solving skills

 - Excellent communication and decision-making abilities

 - High attention to detail and accuracy

 - Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines



What You Can Expect

 - Commitment: 45+ hours per week with flexibility to meet business needs

 - Growth Opportunity: We currently manage 95+ Airbnb properties and are scaling toward 300+ properties this year

 - Performance-Based Bonuses: Competitive bonuses tied to individual and company performance

 - High-Impact Role: You will be empowered to take ownership of all financial processes and help drive the company’s next stage of growth



Why Join Us

We’re a fast-growing, operations-focused Airbnb business committed to excellence in guest experience and backend systems. As our CFO, you will play a critical role in building a financially strong and scalable business. You’ll be part of a team that values accountability, innovation, and long-term growth.



How to Apply

 - Please submit the following:

 - Your RESUME

 - A VOICE RECORDING explaining the experience you will bring to the company and sharing your favorite pastime or hobby



SEND ME A
, DO NOT RESPOND TO THIS.

title: OPERATIONS MANAGER – ECOMMERCE/DROPSHIPPING (FULL TIME)
description:
We operate a high-volume ecommerce dropshipping business across multiple products and markets.



The business is growing. Each department now has a lead. KPIs and reporting exist. Output is strong.



What we need now is not growth generation.



We need structure, stability, and operational control.



We are looking for an Operations Manager to protect and strengthen the backend of the business so founders can focus on strategic growth.



ROLE OBJECTIVE



Your role is defensive and structural.



You are responsible for making sure:

- The machine runs smoothly

- Departments stay aligned

- KPIs are tracked and acted upon

- Reporting gives clarity (not noise)

- Systems are organized and scalable

- Nothing breaks operationally



You are not responsible for generating revenue.

You are responsible for protecting and optimizing operations.



CORE RESPONSIBILITIES



• Oversee daily operations across departments (Creative, Media Buying, CS, etc.)

• Improve and standardize KPI tracking and EOD reporting

• Redesign reporting formats to provide clear visibility for founders

• Build and structure a proper project management system (ClickUp or equivalent)

• Organize SOPs into a centralized, scalable system

• Identify inefficiencies and fix them

• Implement workflow automations when relevant

• Monitor execution discipline and accountability

• Handle operational issues and urgent situations when needed

• Support onboarding of new tea
mbers

• Ensure bonus/KPI logic is applied consistently



WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR



Hard Skills:

- Proven experience in ecommerce operations (agency or high-volume store preferred)

- Strong understanding of performance marketing structure

- Advanced Google Sheets / Excel

- Dashboard creation & KPI modeling

- Workflow optimization

- Experience building systems (ClickUp, Notion, Asana, etc.)

- Comfortable managing remote teams



Soft Skills:

- Calm under pressure

- Structured thinker

- High ownership mentality

- Strong problem solver

- Direct communicator

- Emotionally stable

- Able to handle responsibility without drama



THIS ROLE IS NOT FOR:



- Beginners

- Pure project managers without ecommerce experience

- People who need constant supervision

- People who avoid hard conversations

- People looking for a “chill” job



COMPENSATION



Monthly compensation: $1,600 – $2,200 depending on experience and operational maturity.



Long-term growth and increased compensation possible based on performance and responsibility.



This is a long-term role.



WORK STRUCTURE



Full-time

Remote

Flexible structure, but high accountability



HOW TO APPLY



1. Change your
 subject to:

Let's build the machine



2. Send your CV



3. (Optional but highly recommended) Loom video explaining:

- What you would improve first in a 7-figure ecommerce operation

- How you structure KPIs across multiple departments

- One operational mistake you’ve seen ecommerce founders make



Only applications that follow the instructions will be reviewed.



If you are highly structured, operationally strong, and want to build something long-term, apply.

About the Role:



We’re looking for a detail-oriented Data Operations Specialist to help maintain the accuracy and integrity of our database. This role goes beyond basic data entry. You’ll be responsible for reviewing records, identifying inconsistencies, correcting errors, and ensuring information across systems is accurate, complete, and up to date.



If you enjoy working with structured data, following clear processes, and catching issues before they become problems, this role is a great fit.

title: Business Development Virtual Assisant
description:
Title: Remote Virtual Assistant – LinkedIn Outreach & Administrative Support (Fluent English)



Interstate Logistics Partners (ILP) is hiring a remote virtual assistant to support business development and daily administrative tasks. This role will start as a weekly contract position with the opportunity to grow into a long-term or full-time role based on performance.



We are a logistics company that helps businesses reduce freight costs and improve shipping efficiency. Your primary role will be reaching out to companies on LinkedIn to introduce our consulting approach and schedule consultations with the owner.



This role is remote and we are preferably hiring candidates outside of the United States.



Required Availability

Monday – Friday

7:00 AM – 5:00 PM Eastern Standard Time (EST)



Primary Responsibilities (Main Focus)



Conduct LinkedIn outreach to business owners, operations managers, and supply chain professionals



Send connection requests and start conversations with prospective companies



Introduce ILP’s freight consulting approach



Schedule consultation calls with the owner using a calendar link



Track outreach activity and conversations in a spreadsheet or CRM



Secondary Responsibilities



Answer inbound phone calls when the owner is unavailable



Assist with small administrative tasks



Help organize spreadsheets and business records



Support basic dispatch or operational tasks if needed



About the Service

Interstate Logistics Partners helps businesses review their shipping operations and reduce freight overhead. The owner has 5+ years of experience coordinating freight across the United States and Canada, and consultations are designed to identify opportunities to improve efficiency and lower transportation costs.



Requirements



Fluent English with clear and professional communication



Comfortable speaking with business owners and professionals



Professional presentation and confident communication style



Comfortable appearing on camera when needed



Experience with LinkedIn, social media outreach, or lead generation is a plus



Strong organization and attention to detail



Reliable internet connection and quiet workspace



Application Requirements

To be considered, please include:



A professional headshot



A link to your LinkedIn profile



A short introduction describing your remote work experience



Confirmation that you are available during the required hours



Compensation



Weekly contract to start



Opportunity to grow into a long-term or full-time role based on performance



We are looking for someone who is organized, professional, confident communicating with business leaders, and comfortable representing our company in client-facing interactions.

title: Marketing & Content Manager (Remote — Offshore)
description:
Job Title: Marketing & Content Manager — Healthcare Compliance Company (Remote)

Company: Access Plus Health

Location: Remote (Philippines, Latin America, or Eastern Europe preferred)

Type: Full-time contractor  |  $1,500–$2,500 /month depending on experience

Hours: 40 hours/week  |  Must work US EST or PST hours



About Access Plus Health

Access Plus Health is a physician-led compliance company that provides medical directorship, collaborative practice agreements, and regulatory compliance services to med spas, aesthetics clinics, IV therapy practices, and NP/PA-led businesses across all 50 U.S. states. Founded by a Harvard-trained, board-certified dermatologist, we are building the premium brand in healthcare compliance — and we're growing fast.



The Role

We're looking for a sharp, self-motivated Marketing & Content Manager to own our entire marketing engine — from writing SEO-optimized articles and social media posts to running paid ad campaigns, managing
 marketing, and tracking lead generation metrics. This is a combined marketing execution and content creation role. You'll be the person who makes sure our content gets written and published, our campaigns run, our leads get captured, and our data stays clean.



You'll be writing long-form SEO articles, LinkedIn posts,
 campaigns, and ad copy about U.S. healthcare compliance topics. You don't need to be a healthcare expert on day one — we'll provide the clinical and regulatory knowledge — but you need to be an excellent writer in English who can research topics thoroughly and produce clear, authoritative content. Proficiency with AI writing tools (ChatGPT, Claude, etc.) to accelerate your research and drafting is expected — but every piece of content must be reviewed, edited, and refined by you to ensure accuracy, quality, and originality. We do NOT want AI-generated content; we want AI-assisted content that sounds human, authoritative, and specific.



This is NOT a strategy-only role. You will be executing daily — writing articles, publishing content, managing campaigns, updating the CRM, pulling reports, and optimizing performance. If you love creating content and watching metrics improve, this role is for you.



What You'll Do

Content Creation & SEO (20% of time): Write 2–3 long-form SEO articles per week (1,200–2,500 words) on healthcare compliance topics: CPOM compliance by state, medical directorship requirements, NP/PA supervision, Good Faith Exams, MSO/PC structures, and related topics. We'll provide topic briefs, regulatory source material, and the founder's clinical perspective — you research, draft, edit, and publish. Optimize all content for search (keyword targeting, header structure, meta descriptions, internal linking). Manage the editorial calendar and ensure consistent publishing cadence.



Social Media (20% of time): Write and schedule 3–5 LinkedIn posts per week in the founder's voice (you'll be given talking points and brand guidelines). Manage our presence in relevant
 groups for 
, PAs, and medspa owners. Monitor engagement and respond to comments/DMs. Create short-form content from longer articles (pull quotes, key takeaways, mini-threads).



Referral partnerships / direct outreach (20% of the time): Engage in relevant
 groups, conference groups, organizations, attorney partnerships to engage directly with clients.  

 Marketing (15% of time): Write
 campaigns and automated nurture sequences. Draft webinar promotion
s, follow-up sequences, and monthly newsletters. Segment lists, A/B test subject lines, track open/click rates.



Paid Advertising (10% of time): Set up and manage
, Instagram, and LinkedIn ad campaigns targeting healthcare professionals. Write ad copy. Monitor spend, optimize targeting, and report on cost per lead weekly.



Webinar Support (10% of time): Handle end-to-end logistics for monthly webinars — registration page setup,
 promotion, calendar invites, attendee tracking, post-event follow-up, and content repurposing (pulling clips, creating social posts from key 
nts).



CRM, Reporting & Referral Coordination (5% of time): Ensure all inbound leads are captured in GoHighLevel with proper source attribution. Maintain clean data. Generate weekly reports on lead volume, source, and pipeline progression. Support outreach to referral partners (healthcare attorneys, medspa consultants). Track referral activity.



What We're Looking For

Required: 3+ years of experience in digital marketing with a content focus (agency or in-house). Excellent written English — this is the most important qualification. You will be writing thousands of words per week that need to sound natural, authoritative, and professional to a U.S. healthcare audience. We will test your writing extensively before hiring. Strong SEO skills (keyword research, on-page optimization, content publishing workflows, link-building awareness). Hands-on experience with
 marketing platforms (GoHighLevel, ActiveCampaign, Mailchimp, or similar). Experience running paid social campaigns 
 Ads Manager and/or LinkedIn Campaign Manager). Proficiency with AI writing tools — you know how to use them to accelerate research and drafting while maintaining quality and originality. Proficiency with WordPress or similar CMS. Highly organized, detail-oriented, and self-directed — you'll be managing multiple channels simultaneously. Comfortable with data — you should be able to pull a report and tell us what's working.



Nice to Have: Experience marketing healthcare, compliance, or professional services (even if outside the U.S.). Familiarity with GoHighLevel specifically. Experience with webinar platforms (Zoom Webinars, Demio). Basic graphic design skills (Canva is fine). Understanding of U.S. healthcare terminology (NP, PA, CPOM, medical director, etc.) — or demonstrated ability to learn technical domains quickly.



What We Offer

$1,500–$2,500/month (based on experience) with performance bonuses tied to lead generation targets. Fully remote — work from anywhere. Direct access to the founder — you're not buried in a corporate hierarchy. Opportunity to grow into a marketing leadership role as the company scales. You'll be building a marketing function from the ground up — high impact, high visibility.



How to Apply

Send a brief message (not a generic cover letter) explaining: (1) your relevant marketing and content writing experience, (2) a specific campaign or project you executed that drove measurable results, (3) the tools/platforms you're most proficient with, and (4) complete this writing test:



WRITING TEST (required — applications without this will not be reviewed):

Write a 300–400 word intro and first section for a blog post titled: 'Do You Need a Medical Director for Your Med Spa? A State-by-State Guide.' Your audience is a medspa owner or nurse practitioner who is opening a clinic and isn't sure what compliance requirements apply to them. The tone should be authoritative but accessible — not legalese, not fluffy. You may use AI tools to research, but the writing must be yours — we will check. Include a meta description (under 160 characters) and 3 suggested subheadings for the rest of the article.

title: Sales Development Rep / SDR (Remote — Offshore)
description:
Job Title: Sales Development Representative (SDR) — Healthcare Compliance (Remote)

Company: Access Plus Health

Location: Remote (Philippines or Latin America preferred)

Type: Full-time contractor  |  $1,000–$1,500 /month + performance bonuses

Hours: 40 hours/week  |  Must work during U.S. business hours (Eastern or Pacific time zone)



About Access Plus Health

Access Plus Health is a physician-led compliance company providing medical directorship, collaborative practice agreements, and regulatory compliance services to healthcare businesses across all 50 U.S. states. Founded by a Harvard-trained, board-certified dermatologist, we serve med spas, aesthetics clinics, IV therapy practices, and nurse practitioner-led businesses. We are scaling rapidly and building the sales infrastructure to match our growth.



The Role

We're hiring our first SDR to support the founder (and eventually a dedicated U.S.-based closer) in converting inbound leads into clients. Your job is to make our sales team more efficient by handling everything that isn't the actual selling conversation: qualifying leads, booking discovery calls, managing the CRM, assembling proposals, following up, and keeping the pipeline clean.

You will NOT be cold-calling or doing hard sales. Our leads come in through our website, webinars, referrals, and social media. Your job is to be the organized, responsive, detail-oriented engine that ensures no lead falls through the cracks and every qualified prospect gets on a call.

When you are NOT engaging in these items, your job is to remain proactive.  Be creative, find leads, be hungry. 



What You'll Do

Lead Qualification: Review every inbound lead within 2 hours. Assess fit using our qualification criteria (practice type, state, number of providers, services offered, timeline). Tag and score leads in GoHighLevel. Route qualified leads to the sales calendar.

Appointment Setting: Reach out to qualified leads via
 (using provided templates) to schedule discovery calls. Manage the founder's (and later, closer's) sales calendar. Send confirmation
s, reminders, and prep notes before each call. Reschedule no-shows within 24 hours.

CRM Management: Maintain GoHighLevel pipeline with accurate deal stages, notes, and next steps for every active lead. Update deal values, close dates, and contact information. Generate weekly pipeline reports (total leads, qualified leads, calls booked, calls completed, proposals sent, deals in progress).

Proposal & Document Support: Assemble customized proposals using our templates (fill in client-specific details: state, practice type, number of providers, recommended tier). Prepare compliance assessment memos from discovery call notes. Send proposals to prospects and track open/view status.

Follow-Up: Execute systematic follow-up sequences for leads at every stage — new leads who haven't booked a call, prospects who've had a discovery call but haven't received a proposal, proposals that have been sent but not signed, and past prospects who said 'not now' (quarterly re-engagement). No lead goes more than 48 hours without a next step.

Webinar & Event Follow-Up: After every webinar, contact all registrants within 24 hours. Qualify attendees. Book discovery calls for hot leads. Add warm leads to nurture sequences.

Referral Partner Support: Track referral partner activity. Follow up with referred leads within 24 hours. Report referral conversions monthly.



What We're Looking For

Required: 3+ years of experience in a B2B sales support, SDR, or virtual assistant role (experience working for a U.S.-based company strongly preferred). Excellent written English — your
s need to sound natural and professional to U.S. healthcare professionals. Experience with a CRM platform (GoHighLevel preferred; Salesforce, Pipedrive, or similar accepted). Highly organized and detail-oriented — you manage dozens of leads simultaneously and nothing slips. Fast response time — you treat a new lead like a ticking clock. Comfortable learning a new industry — you don't need to know healthcare compliance today, but you need to learn it quickly and speak about it credibly in
.



Nice to Have: Previous experience in healthcare, compliance, legal, or professional services sales support. Experience with appointment scheduling tools (Calendly, GoHighLevel Meetings). Experience writing or customizing proposals. Familiarity with LinkedIn for professional outreach.



Compensation & Bonuses

$1,000–$1,500 /month base (based on experience). Performance bonuses: $ per qualified discovery call that is booked and attended. Monthly bonus ($200) for hitting lead response time and CRM accuracy targets. 



How to Apply

Send: (1) a brief message explaining your relevant experience in sales support or lead management, (2) a description of the CRM platforms you've used and how you kept pipeline data organized, and (3) complete this test:



 TEST (required — applications without this will not be reviewed):

Pretend you work for Access Plus Health. A med spa owner named Sarah attended our webinar on 'CPOM Compliance for Med Spas' two days ago but hasn't booked a discovery call. You know she runs a 3-location medspa chain in Texas and Florida. Write the follow-up
 to get her on a call (5–8 sentences). Then explain in 2–3 sentences why you wrote it the way you did.

description:

-To transition from manual, outdated processes into scalable digital systems.

-Organize, streamline and automate internal processes to become repeatable.

-Expanding our catering client base by converting 3rd party App customers to direct-ordering customers.

-A tech-savvy, systems-minded manager who can help build, manage and support our operational infrastructure.

-A proactive EVA interested in growing with our U.S.-based catering company.

-A high-level generalist who can provide operational and technical support

-An adaptable Executive Assistant with some marketing awareness (A SMM VA will be hired as well)





Minimum Requirements:

-2+ years VA experience

-Advanced English communication (C1–C2)

-College (Bachelor of Science degree)

-Strong organization & problem-solving skills

-Reliable internet (with backup)

-SOPs

 capture and follow-ups

Starting Rate: $6–$10 USD/hour (based on experience)

30+ hours per week

Below is the link to our application. Please fill out and
 to

About Foodinis Catering

Foodinis is a U.S.-based catering company serving New Jersey since 2007. We specialize in corporate & private drop-off catering and fully staffed events. We are a small company, with less than $500,000.00 in annual sales. Chef/Owner/Operator, John Deatcher wore the Chef Toke along with most of the operational hats for Foodinis over the years and struggled finding time to keep up with the ever-changing world of digital systems. John is excited to bring Foodinis to the next level by working with a highly organized remote assistant who will become his right hand operations and systems manager.

description:
Please only send your application to 

Applications submitted through other platforms or sent to other

We are hiring an E-commerce & Growth Manager to own day-to-day performance of our Shopify store and all external marketplaces. This role requires strong trading discipline, performance focus, and the ability to drive revenue growth across multiple digital channels.



What you’ll do:

title: Social Media Agency - Operations Manager
description:
*Applications that do not follow the instructions at the end will not be assessed*



--------------------------------------------------

ABOUT US

--------------------------------------------------



We run a high-performing social media business in the subscription space. The operation already generates significant volume and our standards are high. Right now our social media arm is overloaded and we are expanding the team quickly so the system can scale.



This role exists to build and manage an offshore execution team that can take pressure off the core operation.



--------------------------------------------------

THE ROLE

--------------------------------------------------



We are hiring a Social Media Operations Manager to build and manage an offshore social media team for one of our clients.



This is an execution role. You will be responsible for hiring editors and social media managers, onboarding them, and managing their output.



This is not a strategy role. It is an operations role focused on hiring, delegation, and delivery. You do however need a solid understanding of social media platforms, short-form content workflows, and the tools used to publish and manage content.



--------------------------------------------------

RESPONSIBILITIES

--------------------------------------------------



• Hiring and onboarding 3+ editors and 3+ SMMs within 90 days, with the first hires live within 30 days

• Overseeing daily content production and posting workflows

• Identifying bottlenecks and process failures

• Creating practical SOPs so work can be repeated consistently

• Setting performance expectations and holding the team accountable

• Managing offshore team communication across time zones

• Maintaining a culture where feedback is normal and not treated as a threat

• Handling performance conversations calmly and professionally



--------------------------------------------------

WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

--------------------------------------------------



Within 90 days the following should be true:



• Editing and posting are fully delegated

• Offshore team is fully staffed and stable

• Clear KPIs are in place

• Content output is predictable and consistent



If these outcomes are not achieved, the role is not working.



--------------------------------------------------

REQUIREMENTS (NON-NEGOTIABLE)

--------------------------------------------------



• Proven experience hiring and managing offshore teams, especially in the Philippines

• Strong English communication and emotional maturity

• Comfortable receiving direct feedback without becoming defensive

• Experience managing remote digital or content teams

• Comfortable working in a fast-moving environment

• A strong understanding of how to use AI to benefit you

• Able to overlap with Australian time zones and work 5–6 days per week



--------------------------------------------------

THIS ROLE IS NOT

--------------------------------------------------



• A creative director position

• A passive coordinator role

• Suited to someone who needs heavy structure

• Primarily a content strategy role



This role is about execution and delivery.



--------------------------------------------------

COMPENSATION

--------------------------------------------------



Up to $9/hr DOE.



Full-time role (usually 40 hours per week initially may be 48).



We pay for reliability and performance and include performance-based bonuses.



--------------------------------------------------

APPLICATION INSTRUCTIONS (REQUIRED)

--------------------------------------------------



To be considered, you must complete all steps below.



Write a short explanation covering the following:



• A team you helped build

• How you hired them

• One mistake you made managing staff and how you fixed it

• Your understanding of the social media industry



Answer this scenario in writing:



We need to hire an editor and a poster within 30 days. Outline your plan: where you would source candidates, how you would screen them, and what your hiring timeline would look like.



Use the subject line: SM Ops Team



We value attention to detail and the ability to follow instructions.



--------------------------------------------------

title: Remote Podcast & Social Media Content Assistant
description:
Part-time | 10–20 hours per week (remote)

Compensation: $7/hour depending on experience

Start Date: Within 2–3 weeks (paid trial required)



THE OPPORTUNITY

We are seeking a highly organized Podcast & Social Media Content Assistant to support the production, distribution, and management of digital content for a YouTube-based podcast.



This role focuses on ensuring episodes, clips, and social media content are consistently published across multiple platforms including YouTube, Instagram, TikTok, and podcast audio platforms.



You will also assist with managing social media posting, engagement, and overall content organization across these channels.



The ideal candidate is detail-oriented, comfortable working with content scheduling tools, and able to manage multiple publishing tasks each week while maintaining quality and consistency.



WHAT YOU’LL BE SUPPORTING

Our podcast produces long-form episodes that are repurposed into short-form clips and distributed across multiple social platforms.



Your role will help ensure episodes, clips, captions, thumbnails, and uploads are organized and scheduled correctly so content goes live consistently.



You will also assist with maintaining our broader social media presence by helping manage posts, captions, and content scheduling across platforms.



THE ROLE

Core Responsibilities

• Upload and schedule full podcast episodes to YouTube

• Upload short clips to Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts

• Write or format captions, descriptions, and hashtags

• Schedule posts using social scheduling tools

• Assist with creating or formatting YouTube thumbnails in Canva

• Organize episode assets including video files, thumbnails, and captions

• Maintain a weekly content calendar

• Coordinate guest scheduling and confirmations

• Ensure content is published on time each week



Social Media Responsibilities



• Assist with managing social media accounts (Instagram, TikTok, YouTube)

• Schedule and organize non-podcast content such as posts, graphics, or announcements

• Monitor comments and messages and flag items that require response

• Help track content performance and identify clips that perform well

• Maintain a structured content calendar across platforms



Additional Responsibilities May Include



• Light video editing for short clips

• Formatting show notes and episode descriptions

• Uploading audio versions to podcast platforms

• Organizing files in Google Drive or Dropbox

• Monitoring posting status and flagging issues quickly



TOOLS YOU MAY USE

• YouTube Studio

• Instagram and TikTok publishing tools

• Planable or similar scheduling platforms

• Canva

• Google Drive or Dropbox



Editing tools such as CapCut, Premiere Pro, Final Cut, or Descript may also be used.



REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

• Experience scheduling and publishing content on social platforms

• Familiarity with YouTube Studio and short-form content posting

• Comfortable using Canva for thumbnails or simple graphics

• Strong written English for captions and descriptions

• Organized and able to manage deadlines

• Reliable internet connection and ability to communicate regularly



NICE TO HAVE

• Experience working with podcasts or YouTube channels

• Understanding of YouTube SEO (titles, tags, descriptions)

• Experience repurposing long-form content into short clips

• Familiarity with podcast hosting platforms such as Spotify for Podcasters, Buzzsprout, or Libsyn



EXAMPLE WEEKLY DELIVERABLES

Typical weekly workflow includes:



• Upload and schedule 1 full YouTube episode

• Prepare and schedule 3–8 short clips and additional social media posts across Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts

• Ensure captions, hashtags, and thumbnails are ready before publishing time

• Coordinate guest scheduling and maintain the podcast calendar



HOW TO APPLY

Please submit:

1. Resume

2. Links to social media accounts, YouTube channels, or content you have managed

3. One YouTube thumbnail you have designed

4. One short-form video clip you edited (YouTube Shorts / IG Reels / TikTok)

5. One caption example you would write for a podcast clip



Please use the subject line: Podcast VA



Only qualified candidates will be contacted for an interview.

title: Junior Data Analyst
description:
Spend smart. Scale fast. Build campaigns that convert at every touchpoint.

Department: Performance Marketing

Type: Part-Time | Remote

Reports To: Head of Performance Marketing/ CEO

Compensation: Competitive Base 



ABOUT AJ MEDIA

We run performance marketing for a portfolio of ecommerce brands — and we're scaling fast. We believe the best media buyers aren't made in agencies. They're made by smart, driven people who treat data like a puzzle and refuse to stop until they've cracked it. If you graduated near the top of your class, you love winning, and you want to get paid to think — this role was built for you.



THE OPPORTUNITY

We don't need five years of experience. We need someone who is sharp, competitive, and hungry. You'll be trained directly by senior media buyers managing millions in ad spend across Taboola, Outbrain, and Revcontent. From day one, you'll be in the data — monitoring campaigns, finding patterns, and making decisions that move the numbers. The best performers here grow into Senior Media Buyers fast.



WHAT YOU'LL DO



Monitor daily spend, CPAs, CTRs, and ROAS — proactively flagging trends before they become problems

Launch and optimize native ad campaigns across Taboola, Outbrain, and related platforms

Run A/B tests on creatives, headlines, thumbnails, and landing pages — and draw clear conclusions from the data

Monitor daily/weekly performance reports and dashboards.

Research competitor campaigns and surface new angles and opportunities

Support senior buyers in audience segmentation, budget pacing, and campaign strategy

Stay sharp on native ad trends, platform updates, and industry best practices





WHO YOU ARE

We hire for ceiling, not resume. The ideal candidate is:



A top student — we're talking Dean's List, cum laude, or consistent honors from a leading Philippine university. Academic excellence signals the discipline and analytical rigor we need.

A numbers person — you get excited by spreadsheets, not intimidated by them. You find it satisfying to chase down why a metric moved.

Competitive and driven — you hate losing more than you love winning. You set your own bar higher than anyone else does.

A fast learner with grit — you don't wait for perfect conditions to start. You figure things out, ask smart questions, and never stop at 'good enough.'

Detail-obsessed — you notice when numbers don't add up, and you don't move on until you understand why.

Clear communicator — you can translate complex data into a one-line insight that drives a decision.



Experience in paid media is a plus — not a requirement. We will train the right person from zero.



BONUS POINTS IF YOU HAVE

Hands-on experience with Taboola, Outbrain, MGID, or any paid media platform

Familiarity with tracking tools: Voluum, RedTrack, or similar

Google Analytics or comparable analytics platform experience

Knowledge of advertorial formats and native ad copywriting

Comfortable with pixels, redirects, and basic web infrastructure



WHY JOIN US

Learn directly fro
dia buyers with $MM in ad spend under management

Real performance data from day one — no busywork, no busy-being-busy

Clear, fast path to Senior Media Buyer for top performers

Access to premium tools, industry courses, and training resources

Performance incentives tied directly to campaign results



Sound like you? Apply here: 
 with your CV and a video introduction on why you're the right person for this role. We read every application personally. No recruiters, no generic forms — just a real conversation about whether we're a fit.

title: GovCon VA
description:
Job Title: Portal Compliance Manager

Company: Team Integrity Knowledge Center (TIKC)

Position Type: Full-Time

Schedule: Monday–Friday, 8:00 AM – 2:00 PM CST (Remote)



About Us



Team Integrity Knowledge Center (TIKC) is a high-impact government contracting consulting firm that helps small businesses build, grow, and scale by becoming fully contract-ready. We specialize in helping clients navigate registrations, certifications, and compliance to compete confidently in the public sector.



We’re expanding our operations and seeking a detail-oriented Government Certification & Registration Specialist to manage client certifications, registrations, and compliance documentation across federal, state, and local portals. If you love structure, accuracy, and helping businesses get “contract-ready,” this role is made for you.



Position Overview



The Government Certification & Registration Specialist will handle end-to-end registration and certification processes for small business clients pursuing government contracts. You’ll work within portals like 
, SBA, state procurement systems, and local certification agencies, ensuring that every client is properly registered, compliant, and positioned for success.



Your role bridges compliance and client experience — ensuring that every registration, renewal, and certification submission is completed correctly, on time, and with integrity.



Key Responsibilities



Manage and complete 
 registrations and renewals, including Entity Validation (via 
 and 
).



Prepare and submit federal certifications such as WOSB, EDWOSB, SDVOSB, HUBZone, 8(a), and others through the SBA certification portal.



Assist clients in obtaining state and local certifications, including MBE, WBE, DBE, and SBE certifications.



Register clients on state procurement portals, such as Georgia Procurement Registry, Texas Comptroller, Florida MyVendorLink, and others.



Maintain and update company and client profiles across multiple systems to ensure accuracy and compliance.



Track certification expirations and coordinate renewals in advance.



Collaborate with clients to collect, review, and organize required documentation (corporate, financial, and operational).



Provide guidance to clients on next steps for becoming “procurement ready.”



Prepare internal summaries or reports on certification status, renewal timelines, and portal access.



Utilize ChatGPT and internal AI tools to streamline communication, generate checklists, and support workflow efficiency.



Maintain confidentiality and integrity in handling all client and company information.



Qualifications



Proven experience in 
 registration and maintenance (Entity Validation, UEI, CAGE code setup).



Strong understanding of federal and state certification processes (WOSB, SDVOSB, HUBZone, 8(a), DBE, MBE, etc.).



Expert in compliance documents and quality control for all certifications 



Familiarity with state procurement systems and vendor registration portals.



Excellent organizational and document management skills with extreme attention to detail.



Ability to manage multiple client files and deadlines simultaneously.



Strong written and verbal communication in English.



Comfortable working independently and problem-solving technical or procedural issues.



Reliable internet connection with backup access.



Integrity, professionalism, and accountability in all interactions.



Preferred Skills (Nice to Have)



Experience working with government contracting consultants or proposal teams.



Knowledge of federal acquisition processes and vendor compliance.



Familiarity with NAICS codes, PSC codes, and set-aside eligibility.



Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and PDF management tools.



Experience supporting clients in industries such as transportation, logistics, or professional services.



Why Join TIKC



At TIKC, you’ll do more than manage paperwork — you’ll help small businesses unlock access to millions in government opportunities. You’ll be part of a mission-driven team that values integrity, efficiency, and client success.



We invest in people who take pride in their work, love structure, and thrive on accuracy. Your attention to detail will help our clients confidently step into the world of government contracting.



How to Apply



If you’re ready to make a real impact helping businesses achieve government compliance and certification success, submit your resume/application here: 

Schedule Your Interview: 



Please include a brief summary of your experience with 
 or SBA certification processes in your
.
